You and the user share the same workspace and collaborate to achieve the user's goals.\n\n# Personality\n\nYou are a deeply pragmatic, effective software engineer. Usage of these comments should be rare.\n- Try to use apply_patch for single file edits, but it is fine to explore other options to make the edit if it does not work well. Findings must be the primary focus of the response - keep summaries or overviews brief and only after enumerating the issues. Present findings first (ordered by severity with file/line references), follow with open questions or assumptions, and offer a change-summary only as a secondary detail. If no findings are discovered, state that explicitly and mention any residual risks or testing gaps.\n\n## Frontend tasks\n\nWhen doing frontend design tasks, avoid collapsing into \"AI slop\" or safe, average-looking layouts.\nAim for interfaces that feel intentional, bold, and a bit surprising.\n- Typography: Use expressive, purposeful fonts and avoid default stacks (Inter, Roboto, Arial, system).\n- Color & Look: Choose a clear visual direction; define CSS variables; avoid purple-on-white defaults. No purple bias or dark mode bias.\n- Motion: Use a few meaningful animations (page-load, staggered reveals) instead of generic micro-motions.\n- Background: Don't rely on flat, single-color backgrounds; use gradients, shapes, or subtle patterns to build atmosphere.\n- Overall: Avoid boilerplate layouts and interchangeable UI patterns. Vary themes, type families, and visual languages across outputs.\n- Ensure the page loads properly on both desktop and mobile\n\nException: If working within an existing website or design system, preserve the established patterns, structure, and visual language.\n\n# Working with the user\n\nYou interact with the user through a terminal. For numbered lists, only use the `1. 2. 3.` style markers (with a period), never `1)`.\n- Headers are optional, only use them when you think they are necessary. If you do use them, use short Title Case (1-3 words) wrapped in **…**. Don't add a blank line.\n- Use monospace commands/paths/env vars/code ids, inline examples, and literal keyword bullets by wrapping them in backticks.\n- Code samples or multi-line snippets should be wrapped in fenced code blocks. Include an info string as often as possible.\n- File References: When referencing files in your response follow the below rules:\n  * Use markdown links (not inline code) for clickable files.\n  * Each file reference should have a stand-alone path; use inline code for non-clickable paths (for example, directories).\n  * For clickable/openable file references, the path target must be an absolute filesystem path. Labels may be short (for example, `[app.ts](/abs/path/app.ts)`).\n  * Optionally include line/column (1‑based): :line[:column] or #Lline[Ccolumn] (column defaults to 1).\n  * Do not use URIs like file://, vscode://, or https://.\n  * Do not provide range of lines\n  * Examples: src/app.ts, src/app.ts:42, b/server/index.js#L10, REDACTED\n- Don’t use emojis or em dashes unless explicitly instructed.\n\n## Final answer instructions\n\n- Balance conciseness to not overwhelm the user with appropriate detail for the request. Do not narrate abstractly; explain what you are doing and why.\n- Do not begin responses with conversational interjections or meta commentary. Avoid openers such as acknowledgements (“Done —”, “Got it”, “Great question, ”) or framing phrases.\n- The user does not see command execution outputs. When asked to show the output of a command (e.g. `git show`), relay the important details in your answer or summarize the key lines so the user understands the result.\n- Never tell the user to \"save/copy this file\", the user is on the same machine and has access to the same files as you have.\n- If the user asks for a code explanation, structure your answer with code references.\n- When given a simple task, just provide the outcome in a short answer without strong formatting.\n- When you make big or complex changes, state the solution first, then walk the user through what you did and why.\n- For casual chit-chat, just chat.\n- If you weren't able to do something, for example run tests, tell the user.\n- If there are natural next steps the user may want to take, suggest them at the end of your response.           "type": "input_text",
          "text": "<collaboration_mode># Plan Mode (Conversational)\r\n\r\nYou work in 3 phases, and you should *chat your way* to a great plan before finalizing it. A great plan is very detailed—intent- and implementation-wise—so that it can be handed to another engineer or agent to be implemented right away. It must be **decision complete**, where the implementer does not need to make any decisions.\r\n\r\n## Mode rules (strict)\r\n\r\nYou are in **Plan Mode** until a developer message explicitly ends it.\r\n\r\nPlan Mode is not changed by user intent, tone, or imperative language. If a user asks for execution while still in Plan Mode, treat it as a request to **plan the execution**, not perform it.\r\n\r\n## Plan Mode vs update_plan tool\r\n\r\nPlan Mode is a collaboration mode that can involve requesting user input and eventually issuing a `<proposed_plan>` block.\r\n\r\nSeparately, `update_plan` is a checklist/progress/TODOs tool; it does not enter or exit Plan Mode. Do not confuse it with Plan mode or try to use it while in Plan mode. If you try to use `update_plan` in Plan mode, it will return an error.\r\n\r\n## Execution vs. mutation in Plan Mode\r\n\r\nYou may explore and execute **non-mutating** actions that improve the plan. You must not perform **mutating** actions.\r\n\r\n### Allowed (non-mutating, plan-improving)\r\n\r\nActions that gather truth, reduce ambiguity, or validate feasibility without changing repo-tracked state. Examples:\r\n\r\n* Reading or searching files, configs, schemas, types, manifests, and docs\r\n* Static analysis, inspection, and repo exploration\r\n* Dry-run style commands when they do not edit repo-tracked files\r\n* Tests, builds, or checks that may write to caches or build artifacts (for example, `target/`, `.cache/`, or snapshots) so long as they do not edit repo-tracked files\r\n\r\n### Not allowed (mutating, plan-executing)\r\n\r\nActions that implement the plan or change repo-tracked state. Examples:\r\n\r\n* Editing or writing files\r\n* Running formatters or linters that rewrite files\r\n* Applying patches, migrations, or codegen that updates repo-tracked files\r\n* Side-effectful commands whose purpose is to carry out the plan rather than refine it\r\n\r\nWhen in doubt: if the action would reasonably be described as \"doing the work\" rather than \"planning the work,\" do not do it.\r\n\r\n## PHASE 1 — Ground in the environment (explore first, ask second)\r\n\r\nBegin by grounding yourself in the actual environment. Eliminate unknowns in the prompt by discovering facts, not by asking the user. Resolve all questions that can be answered through exploration or inspection. Identify missing or ambiguous details only if they cannot be derived from the environment. Silent exploration between turns is allowed and encouraged.\r\n\r\nBefore asking the user any question, perform at least one targeted non-mutating exploration pass (for example: search relevant files, inspect likely entrypoints/configs, confirm current implementation shape), unless no local environment/repo is available.\r\n\r\nException: you may ask clarifying questions about the user's prompt before exploring, ONLY if there are obvious ambiguities or contradictions in the prompt itself. However, if ambiguity might be resolved by exploring, always prefer exploring first.\r\n\r\nDo not ask questions that can be answered from the repo or system (for example, \"where is this struct?\" or \"which UI component should we use?\" when exploration can make it clear). Only ask once you have exhausted reasonable non-mutating exploration.\r\n\r\n## PHASE 2 — Intent chat (what they actually want)\r\n\r\n* Keep asking until you can clearly state: goal + success criteria, audience, in/out of scope, constraints, current state, and the key preferences/tradeoffs.\r\n* Bias toward questions over guessing: if any high-impact ambiguity remains, do NOT plan yet—ask.\r\n\r\n## PHASE 3 — Implementation chat (what/how we’ll build)\r\n\r\n* Once intent is stable, keep asking until the spec is decision complete: approach, interfaces (APIs/schemas/I/O), data flow, edge cases/failure modes, testing + acceptance criteria, rollout/monitoring, and any migrations/compat constraints.\r\n\r\n## Asking questions\r\n\r\nCritical rules:\r\n\r\n* Strongly prefer using the `request_user_input` tool to ask any questions.\r\n* Offer only meaningful multiple‑choice options; don’t include filler choices that are obviously wrong or irrelevant.\r\n* In rare cases where an unavoidable, important question can’t be expressed with reasonable multiple‑choice options (due to extreme ambiguity), you may ask it directly without the tool.\r\n\r\nYou SHOULD ask many questions, but each question must:\r\n\r\n* materially change the spec/plan, OR\r\n* confirm/lock an assumption, OR\r\n* choose between meaningful tradeoffs.\r\n* not be answerable by non-mutating commands.\r\n\r\nUse the `request_user_input` tool only for decisions that materially change the plan, for confirming important assumptions, or for information that cannot be discovered via non-mutating exploration.\r\n\r\n## Two kinds of unknowns (treat differently)\r\n\r\n1. **Discoverable facts** (repo/system truth): explore first.\r\n\r\n   * Before asking, run targeted searches and check likely sources of truth (configs/manifests/entrypoints/schemas/types/constants).\r\n   * Ask only if: multiple plausible candidates; nothing found but you need a missing identifier/context; or ambiguity is actually product intent.\r\n   * If asking, present concrete candidates (paths/service names) + recommend one.\r\n   * Never ask questions you can answer from your environment (e.g., “where is this struct”).\r\n\r\n2. **Preferences/tradeoffs** (not discoverable): ask early.\r\n\r\n   * These are intent or implementation preferences that cannot be derived from exploration.\r\n   * Provide 2–4 mutually exclusive options + a recommended default.\r\n   * If unanswered, proceed with the recommended option and record it as an assumption in the final plan.\r\n\r\n## Finalization rule\r\n\r\nOnly output the final plan when it is decision complete and leaves no decisions to the implementer.\r\n\r\nWhen you present the official plan, wrap it in a `<proposed_plan>` block so the client can render it specially:\r\n\r\n1) The opening tag must be on its own line.\r\n2) Start the plan content on the next line (no text on the same line as the tag).\r\n3) The closing tag must be on its own line.\r\n4) Use Markdown inside the block.\r\n5) Keep the tags exactly as `<proposed_plan>` and `</proposed_plan>` (do not translate or rename them), even if the plan content is in another language.\r\n\r\nExample:\r\n\r\n<proposed_plan>\r\nplan content\r\n</proposed_plan>\r\n\r\nplan content should be human and agent digestible. The final plan must be plan-only and include:\r\n\r\n* A clear title\r\n* A brief summary section\r\n* Important changes or additions to public APIs/interfaces/types\r\n* Test cases and scenarios\r\n* Explicit assumptions and defaults chosen where needed\r\n\r\nDo not ask \"should I proceed?\" in the final output. The user can easily switch out of Plan mode and request implementation if you have included a `<proposed_plan>` block in your response. Alternatively, they can decide to stay in Plan mode and continue refining the plan.\r\n\r\nOnly produce at most one `<proposed_plan>` block per turn, and only when you are presenting a complete spec.\r\n\r\nIf the user stays in Plan mode and asks for revisions after a prior `<proposed_plan>`, any new `<proposed_plan>` must be a complete replacement.\r\n</collaboration_mode>"
        }
      ]

          "type": "input_text",
          "text": "You are implementing a local, production-style job queue + scheduler system (“mini Celery”) intended to stress-test an AI coding agent’s ability to design reliable concurrent systems.\r\n\r\nDeliver a complete, runnable repository in Python 3.12 with the following properties:\r\n- Clear modular architecture (API, broker abstraction, persistence, worker, scheduler, observability, CLI, tests).\r\n- Deterministic behavior and strong error handling.\r\n- Extensive automated tests (unit + integration).\r\n- One-command local startup via Docker Compose.\r\n\r\nTechnology constraints (use exactly these unless impossible, then document the exception in README):\r\n- Language: Python 3.12\r\n- API: FastAPI + Uvicorn\r\n- Broker: Redis (use Redis Streams + consumer groups for reliable delivery)\r\n- Persistence: SQLite (SQLModel or SQLAlchemy); store job metadata and state transitions durably\r\n- Tests: pytest\r\n- Formatting/linting: ruff (and mypy if feasible)\r\n- Observability: structured JSON logs + Prometheus metrics endpoint\r\n\r\nSystem overview\r\nBuild three runnable services plus a CLI:\r\n1) API service: accepts job submissions and exposes job status, results, and admin actions.\r\n2) Worker service: consumes jobs from Redis Streams, executes them, records transitions/results, and acknowledges completion.\r\n3) Scheduler service: enqueues delayed jobs and cron-like recurring jobs.\r\n4) CLI: submit jobs, inspect status, tail events, and run local load tests.\r\n\r\nCore concepts and data model\r\nJob states: QUEUED, RUNNING, SUCCEEDED, FAILED, CANCELED, DEAD\r\nRequired tables (minimum):\r\n- jobs: id (UUID), type, payload(JSON), state, created_at, updated_at, idempotency_key (nullable), attempts, max_attempts, next_run_at, schedule (nullable), last_error (nullable), result (nullable JSON)\r\n- job_events: append-only event log (job_id, ts, old_state, new_state, message, metadata JSON)\r\n\r\nBroker semantics (Redis Streams)\r\n- Use a dedicated stream per queue (e.g., stream “jobs:default”) and a consumer group (e.g., “workers”).\r\n- Reliable processing requirements:\r\n  - A job must not be lost if a worker crashes mid-execution.\r\n  - Implement a visibility-timeout style reclaim: periodically scan PEL (pending entries list) and XCLAIM jobs stuck beyond a configurable timeout.\r\n  - A job must be acknowledged (XACK) only after the final state transition is durably persisted.\r\n  - Ensure at-least-once delivery; implement idempotency controls to prevent duplicate side-effects for provided idempotency keys.\r\n\r\nAPI requirements\r\nExpose these endpoints (exact behavior is required):\r\n- POST /jobs\r\n  - Request: { \"type\": string, \"payload\": object, \"queue\": \"default\" (optional), \"max_attempts\": int (optional, default 3), \"run_at\": RFC3339 timestamp (optional), \"idempotency_key\": string (optional) }\r\n  - Behavior:\r\n    - If idempotency_key already exists for a job in non-terminal state, return that job (do not enqueue a new one).\r\n    - If run_at is absent, enqueue immediately; otherwise store as delayed and let scheduler enqueue at run_at.\r\n  - Response: { \"job_id\": uuid }\r\n- GET /jobs/{job_id}\r\n  - Returns full job record including state, attempts, result (if terminal), last_error (if any)\r\n- POST /jobs/{job_id}/cancel\r\n  - If job is QUEUED or delayed (not yet enqueued), mark CANCELED and ensure it will not run\r\n  - If job is RUNNING, mark as “cancel requested”; worker must check a cancellation flag between steps for cooperative cancellation for supported tasks\r\n- POST /jobs/{job_id}/retry\r\n  - Only for FAILED or DEAD jobs; resets state to QUEUED and enqueues again with attempts=0 (or attempts preserved but allowed; choose one and document)\r\n- GET /jobs?state=&type=&limit=&cursor=\r\n  - Basic pagination over jobs\r\n- GET /metrics\r\n  - Prometheus metrics\r\n- GET /healthz\r\n  - Liveness and broker/db connectivity checks\r\n\r\nWorker requirements\r\n- Configurable concurrency: N processes or asyncio tasks (choose one; document)\r\n- Job execution framework:\r\n  - Jobs are registered “task types” (e.g., “sleep”, “http_get”, “cpu_burn”, “fail_then_succeed”, “pipeline_demo”)\r\n  - Each task must validate payload schema (pydantic models)\r\n  - Record state transitions: QUEUED→RUNNING→(SUCCEEDED|FAILED|CANCELED|DEAD)\r\n- Retry policy:\r\n  - Exponential backoff with jitter: base_delay (e.g., 1s), multiplier, cap; configurable per job type\r\n  - Increment attempts on each execution attempt\r\n  - On exceeding max_attempts, mark DEAD and push an entry to a dead-letter stream (e.g., “jobs:dlq”)\r\n- Idempotency:\r\n  - If idempotency_key is provided, ensure that re-delivered jobs do not re-run side effects if they already SUCCEEDED. Return the prior result instead.\r\n- Cooperative cancellation:\r\n  - For at least one task type, implement multi-step execution that checks a cancellation flag between steps.\r\n\r\nScheduler requirements\r\nSupport both:\r\n1) Delayed jobs (run_at): store jobs with next_run_at, and enqueue them when due.\r\n2) Cron-like recurring jobs:\r\n   - API to create schedules is optional, but scheduler must support loading schedules from a config file (YAML/JSON) at startup.\r\n   - Example schedule: every minute enqueue “pipeline_demo” with payload.\r\nImplementation requirements:\r\n- Scheduler must be robust to restarts (no duplicate enqueues beyond at-least-once semantics; use a dedupe key per scheduled tick).\r\n- Store schedule ticks in DB or Redis with a deterministic key (schedule_id + planned_time) to prevent repeated enqueue on restart.\r\n\r\nObservability requirements\r\n- Structured JSON logs for all services with correlation fields:\r\n  - job_id, stream_id, consumer, attempt, state\r\n- Prometheus metrics (minimum):\r\n  - jobs_enqueued_total{type,queue}\r\n  - jobs_started_total{type}\r\n  - jobs_finished_total{type,state}\r\n  - job_duration_seconds histogram{type}\r\n  - queue_depth gauge{queue}\r\n  - worker_reclaims_total\r\n  - scheduler_enqueues_total{schedule_id}\r\n- Include a simple “events tail” in CLI that streams job_events via polling.\r\n\r\nRepository requirements\r\nProvide:\r\n- docker-compose.yml that starts Redis + API + scheduler + at least 1 worker\r\n- Makefile (or just scripts) with:\r\n  - make test\r\n  - make lint\r\n  - make up\r\n  - make down\r\n  - make loadtest (submit many jobs concurrently and report throughput/latency)\r\n- README.md with:\r\n  - Architecture explanation\r\n  - Failure scenarios and how your design handles them (worker crash, Redis restart, scheduler restart)\r\n  - Quickstart commands\r\n  - How to add a new task type\r\n  - Clear list of design tradeoffs\r\n\r\nTesting requirements (must be automated)\r\n- Unit tests:\r\n  - State transition rules\r\n  - Retry backoff calculation (including jitter bounds)\r\n  - Idempotency behavior\r\n- Integration tests (dockerized Redis OK):\r\n  - At-least-once delivery: simulate worker crash mid-job and verify job is reclaimed and completes\r\n  - DLQ behavior after max_attempts\r\n  - Delayed job runs not earlier than run_at\r\n  - Scheduler restart does not enqueue duplicates for the same scheduled tick\r\n- All tests must pass via “make test”.\r\n\r\nDo not ask me questions. If any detail is ambiguous, choose a reasonable default and document it in README under “Assumptions”.\r\nDeliver working code, not pseudocode.\n"
        }
      ]
    },

      "type": "message",

      "content": [
        {
          "type": "output_text",
          "text": "<proposed_plan>\n# Mini Celery (Python 3.12) — Decision-Complete Build Plan\n\n## Summary\n- Build a greenfield repository named `mini_celery` with four runnables: FastAPI API, Redis Streams worker, scheduler, and CLI.\n- Use Redis Streams + consumer groups for at-least-once delivery and reclaim via `XPENDING` + `XCLAIM`.\n- Persist authoritative job state and transition history in SQLite via SQLAlchemy 2.0.\n- Provide deterministic behavior via explicit UTC timestamps, injectable clock, bounded jitter, strict state machine, and structured error paths.\n- Ship Docker Compose startup (`redis`, `api`, `worker`, `scheduler`) plus `make` targets and script fallbacks.\n- Include unit + integration tests that directly cover all required reliability scenarios.\n\n## Repository Layout\n- `pyproject.toml` with dependencies, ruff, pytest, mypy config.\n- `Dockerfile` single image used by API/worker/scheduler/cli.\n- `docker-compose.yml` for local runtime.\n- `docker-compose.test.yml` for isolated integration test execution.\n- `Makefile` with `test`, `lint`, `up`, `down`, `loadtest`.\n- `scripts/dev.py` fallback task runner for environments without `make`.\n- `README.md` with architecture, quickstart, failure scenarios, tradeoffs, assumptions, extensibility.\n- `config/schedules.yaml` recurring schedule examples.\n- `mini_celery/__init__.py`\n- `mini_celery/config.py` env settings.\n- `mini_celery/logging.py` JSON logging setup.\n- `mini_celery/metrics.py` Prometheus counters/gauges/histograms.\n- `mini_celery/db.py` engine/session/init.\n- `mini_celery/models.py` SQLAlchemy models and enums.\n- `mini_celery/schemas.py` Pydantic request/response models.\n- `mini_celery/state_machine.py` allowed transitions + validation.\n- `mini_celery/repository.py` DB operations (jobs, events, idempotency, schedule ticks).\n- `mini_celery/broker/base.py` broker protocol.\n- `mini_celery/broker/redis_streams.py` stream/group operations, reclaim, dlq.\n- `mini_celery/tasks/base.py` task protocol + registry.\n- `mini_celery/tasks/types.py` `sleep`, `http_get`, `cpu_burn`, `fail_then_succeed`, `pipeline_demo`.\n- `mini_celery/worker/main.py` worker service.\n- `mini_celery/scheduler/main.py` delayed + recurring scheduler service.\n- `mini_celery/api/main.py` FastAPI app wiring.\n- `mini_celery/api/routes_jobs.py` jobs/admin/events endpoints.\n- `mini_celery/api/routes_health.py` `/healthz`.\n- `mini_celery/cli.py` submit/status/tail/loadtest.\n- `tests/unit/...`\n- `tests/integration/...`\n- `tests/conftest.py`\n\n## Public APIs, Interfaces, and Types\n- HTTP API (required + one additive endpoint):\n1. `POST /jobs` exact request contract; returns `{ \"job_id\": \"<uuid>\" }`.\n2. `GET /jobs/{job_id}` returns full job record.\n3. `POST /jobs/{job_id}/cancel` supports queued/delayed cancellation and running cancel request.\n4. `POST /jobs/{job_id}/retry` allowed only for `FAILED`/`DEAD`; resets `attempts=0` and requeues.\n5. `GET /jobs?state=&type=&limit=&cursor=` cursor pagination.\n6. `GET /metrics` Prometheus exposition.\n7. `GET /healthz` liveness + Redis + DB checks.\n8. `GET /jobs/{job_id}/events?cursor_ts=` additive endpoint for CLI tail polling.\n- Redis stream message schema:\n- Fields: `job_id`, `type`, `queue`, `enqueued_at`, `dispatch_token`.\n- Streams: `jobs:{queue}` and DLQ stream `jobs:dlq`.\n- Group: `workers`.\n- Scheduler config schema (`config/schedules.yaml`):\n- `id`, `cron`, `queue`, `type`, `payload`, `max_attempts`, `enabled`.\n- CLI commands:\n- `submit`, `status`, `list`, `tail-events`, `loadtest`.\n\n## Data Model\n- Required `jobs` table with all requested columns.\n- Required `job_events` append-only table with requested columns.\n- Added columns in `jobs` for reliability:\n- `queue` (string), `cancel_requested` (bool), `lease_expires_at` (datetime nullable), `last_stream_id` (string nullable), `enqueued` (bool), `version` (int optimistic concurrency).\n- Added table `idempotency_locks`:\n- `key` (PK), `owner_job_id`, `status` (`IN_PROGRESS|SUCCEEDED`), `result` JSON, `updated_at`.\n- Added table `schedule_ticks`:\n- `schedule_id`, `planned_time`, `job_id`, `created_at`; unique `(schedule_id, planned_time)`.\n\n## State Machine and Core Semantics\n- States: `QUEUED`, `RUNNING`, `SUCCEEDED`, `FAILED`, `CANCELED`, `DEAD`.\n- Allowed transitions:\n- `QUEUED -> RUNNING|CANCELED`\n- `RUNNING -> SUCCEEDED|FAILED|CANCELED|DEAD|QUEUED` (queued only for retryable failures/backoff)\n- `FAILED -> QUEUED` (manual retry)\n- `DEAD -> QUEUED` (manual retry)\n- Terminal states: `SUCCEEDED`, `FAILED`, `CANCELED`, `DEAD`.\n- Every transition appends one immutable `job_events` row in same DB transaction as state update.\n\n## Worker Behavior\n- Concurrency model: asyncio tasks (`WORKER_CONCURRENCY`, default `4`) in one process.\n- Consume loop: `XREADGROUP BLOCK` on `jobs:{queue}`.\n- Reclaim loop: every `RECLAIM_INTERVAL_SEC` run `XPENDING` filter by idle > `VISIBILITY_TIMEOUT_SEC`, then `XCLAIM`.\n- Processing order:\n1. Receive stream message.\n2. Load job and atomically claim execution if runnable.\n3. Persist `RUNNING` and increment attempt count.\n4. Execute task with payload validation.\n5. Persist final/retry state + event.\n6. `XACK` only after successful durable persistence of final transition.\n- Retry policy:\n- Backoff `delay = min(cap, base * multiplier^(attempt-1)) + jitter`.\n- Jitter bounded by `jitter_ratio` (default `0.2`) and deterministic in tests via seeded RNG fixture.\n- Retryable failures re-enter `QUEUED` with `next_run_at = now + delay` and `enqueued=false`.\n- Exceeded attempts -> `DEAD` and publish entry to `jobs:dlq`.\n- Idempotency:\n- API dedupe for same key while non-terminal.\n- Worker checks `idempotency_locks` before side effects.\n- If key already `SUCCEEDED`, worker short-circuits and reuses stored result.\n- First executor acquires/owns lock; success stores result in lock row.\n- Cooperative cancellation:\n- `pipeline_demo` executes step-wise and checks `cancel_requested` between steps.\n- On cancellation request during running, transition to `CANCELED` at next checkpoint.\n\n## Scheduler Behavior\n- Delayed jobs:\n- Poll DB for `state=QUEUED`, `enqueued=false`, `next_run_at<=now`, `cancel_requested=false`.\n- Enqueue to Redis stream and set `enqueued=true`, `last_stream_id`.\n- If crash after enqueue before DB update, duplicate enqueue is tolerated; worker CAS logic prevents duplicate execution.\n- Recurring jobs:\n- Load schedules from YAML/JSON on startup.\n- Use croniter in UTC to compute planned ticks.\n- For each planned tick, insert `schedule_ticks` unique key `(schedule_id, planned_time)`.\n- Only successful insert creates job and increments `scheduler_enqueues_total{schedule_id}`.\n- Restart-safe dedupe comes from `schedule_ticks` uniqueness.\n\n## Observability\n- Structured JSON logs for API/worker/scheduler/CLI.\n- Mandatory correlation fields emitted when available: `job_id`, `stream_id`, `consumer`, `attempt`, `state`.\n- Prometheus metrics exposed at `/metrics` with required metrics:\n- `jobs_enqueued_total{type,queue}`\n- `jobs_started_total{type}`\n- `jobs_finished_total{type,state}`\n- `job_duration_seconds{type}` histogram\n- `queue_depth{queue}` gauge\n- `worker_reclaims_total`\n- `scheduler_enqueues_total{schedule_id}`\n\n## Docker, Startup, and Developer Commands\n- `docker-compose.yml` services:\n- `redis` with persistence.\n- `api` on `:8000`.\n- `worker` (default queue `default`).\n- `scheduler` loading `config/schedules.yaml`.\n- Shared volume for SQLite DB path `/app/data/mini_celery.db`.\n- Commands:\n- `make up` -> `docker compose up --build -d`\n- `make down` -> `docker compose down -v`\n- `make lint` -> `ruff check . && ruff format --check . && mypy mini_celery`\n- `make test` -> compose-based test run with Redis + pytest.\n- `make loadtest` -> CLI loadtest against running API.\n- Fallback for no `make`: `python scripts/dev.py <up|down|lint|test|loadtest>`.\n\n## Automated Tests\n- Unit tests:\n- State transition rules including invalid transitions rejection.\n- Backoff/jitter bounds and monotonic growth under cap.\n- API idempotency behavior for non-terminal duplicates.\n- Worker idempotency short-circuit when prior success exists.\n- Integration tests:\n- At-least-once with crash: kill worker mid-job, second worker reclaims from PEL and completes.\n- DLQ after max attempts: retryable task exceeds attempts, ends in `DEAD`, entry appears in `jobs:dlq`.\n- Delayed execution: job with future `run_at` never runs early; runs after due time.\n- Scheduler restart dedupe: same `(schedule_id, planned_time)` enqueued once across restart.\n- `make test` exits non-zero on any failing unit/integration test.\n\n## README Content Plan\n- Architecture diagram and service responsibilities.\n- Reliability walkthrough for worker crash, Redis restart, scheduler restart.\n- Quickstart with exact commands.\n- API examples for each endpoint.\n- How to add a new task type (Pydantic payload + registry + tests).\n- Design tradeoffs section.\n- Assumptions section.\n\n## Assumptions and Defaults\n- UTC-only timestamps; RFC3339 accepted/emitted with timezone.\n- Manual retry resets `attempts` to `0`, clears `last_error`, preserves `id`, `type`, `payload`, `idempotency_key`.\n- Default queue name is `default`.\n- Default `max_attempts=3`.\n- Worker concurrency uses asyncio tasks, not multiprocessing.\n- Backoff defaults: `base=1s`, `multiplier=2.0`, `cap=30s`, `jitter_ratio=0.2`.\n- Visibility timeout default `30s`; reclaim interval default `5s`.\n- SQLite runs in WAL mode with busy timeout to reduce write contention.\n- Extra endpoint `GET /jobs/{job_id}/events` is added to support CLI tailing cleanly.\n- Because this environment lacks local `docker` and `make`, implementation will include script fallbacks while preserving required `make` targets and Docker-first workflow.\n</proposed_plan>"
        }
      ],
      "phase": "final_answer"
    },
    {

      "role": "developer",

          "type": "input_text",
          "text": "<collaboration_mode># Collaboration Mode: Default\r\n\r\nYou are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\r\n\r\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\r\n\r\n## request_user_input availability\r\n\r\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode.           "type": "input_text",
          "text": "PLEASE IMPLEMENT THIS PLAN:\n# Mini Celery (Python 3.12) — Decision-Complete Build Plan\n\n## Summary\n- Build a greenfield repository named `mini_celery` with four runnables: FastAPI API, Redis Streams worker, scheduler, and CLI.\n- Use Redis Streams + consumer groups for at-least-once delivery and reclaim via `XPENDING` + `XCLAIM`.\n- Persist authoritative job state and transition history in SQLite via SQLAlchemy 2.0.\n- Provide deterministic behavior via explicit UTC timestamps, injectable clock, bounded jitter, strict state machine, and structured error paths.\n- Ship Docker Compose startup (`redis`, `api`, `worker`, `scheduler`) plus `make` targets and script fallbacks.\n- Include unit + integration tests that directly cover all required reliability scenarios.\n\n## Repository Layout\n- `pyproject.toml` with dependencies, ruff, pytest, mypy config.\n- `Dockerfile` single image used by API/worker/scheduler/cli.\n- `docker-compose.yml` for local runtime.\n- `docker-compose.test.yml` for isolated integration test execution.\n- `Makefile` with `test`, `lint`, `up`, `down`, `loadtest`.\n- `scripts/dev.py` fallback task runner for environments without `make`.\n- `README.md` with architecture, quickstart, failure scenarios, tradeoffs, assumptions, extensibility.\n- `config/schedules.yaml` recurring schedule examples.\n- `mini_celery/__init__.py`\n- `mini_celery/config.py` env settings.\n- `mini_celery/logging.py` JSON logging setup.\n- `mini_celery/metrics.py` Prometheus counters/gauges/histograms.\n- `mini_celery/db.py` engine/session/init.\n- `mini_celery/models.py` SQLAlchemy models and enums.\n- `mini_celery/schemas.py` Pydantic request/response models.\n- `mini_celery/state_machine.py` allowed transitions + validation.\n- `mini_celery/repository.py` DB operations (jobs, events, idempotency, schedule ticks).\n- `mini_celery/broker/base.py` broker protocol.\n- `mini_celery/broker/redis_streams.py` stream/group operations, reclaim, dlq.\n- `mini_celery/tasks/base.py` task protocol + registry.\n- `mini_celery/tasks/types.py` `sleep`, `http_get`, `cpu_burn`, `fail_then_succeed`, `pipeline_demo`.\n- `mini_celery/worker/main.py` worker service.\n- `mini_celery/scheduler/main.py` delayed + recurring scheduler service.\n- `mini_celery/api/main.py` FastAPI app wiring.\n- `mini_celery/api/routes_jobs.py` jobs/admin/events endpoints.\n- `mini_celery/api/routes_health.py` `/healthz`.\n- `mini_celery/cli.py` submit/status/tail/loadtest.\n- `tests/unit/...`\n- `tests/integration/...`\n- `tests/conftest.py`\n\n## Public APIs, Interfaces, and Types\n- HTTP API (required + one additive endpoint):\n1. `POST /jobs` exact request contract; returns `{ \"job_id\": \"<uuid>\" }`.\n2. `GET /jobs/{job_id}` returns full job record.\n3. `POST /jobs/{job_id}/cancel` supports queued/delayed cancellation and running cancel request.\n4. `POST /jobs/{job_id}/retry` allowed only for `FAILED`/`DEAD`; resets `attempts=0` and requeues.\n5. `GET /jobs?state=&type=&limit=&cursor=` cursor pagination.\n6. `GET /metrics` Prometheus exposition.\n7. `GET /healthz` liveness + Redis + DB checks.\n8. `GET /jobs/{job_id}/events?cursor_ts=` additive endpoint for CLI tail polling.\n- Redis stream message schema:\n- Fields: `job_id`, `type`, `queue`, `enqueued_at`, `dispatch_token`.\n- Streams: `jobs:{queue}` and DLQ stream `jobs:dlq`.\n- Group: `workers`.\n- Scheduler config schema (`config/schedules.yaml`):\n- `id`, `cron`, `queue`, `type`, `payload`, `max_attempts`, `enabled`.\n- CLI commands:\n- `submit`, `status`, `list`, `tail-events`, `loadtest`.\n\n## Data Model\n- Required `jobs` table with all requested columns.\n- Required `job_events` append-only table with requested columns.\n- Added columns in `jobs` for reliability:\n- `queue` (string), `cancel_requested` (bool), `lease_expires_at` (datetime nullable), `last_stream_id` (string nullable), `enqueued` (bool), `version` (int optimistic concurrency).\n- Added table `idempotency_locks`:\n- `key` (PK), `owner_job_id`, `status` (`IN_PROGRESS|SUCCEEDED`), `result` JSON, `updated_at`.\n- Added table `schedule_ticks`:\n- `schedule_id`, `planned_time`, `job_id`, `created_at`; unique `(schedule_id, planned_time)`.\n\n## State Machine and Core Semantics\n- States: `QUEUED`, `RUNNING`, `SUCCEEDED`, `FAILED`, `CANCELED`, `DEAD`.\n- Allowed transitions:\n- `QUEUED -> RUNNING|CANCELED`\n- `RUNNING -> SUCCEEDED|FAILED|CANCELED|DEAD|QUEUED` (queued only for retryable failures/backoff)\n- `FAILED -> QUEUED` (manual retry)\n- `DEAD -> QUEUED` (manual retry)\n- Terminal states: `SUCCEEDED`, `FAILED`, `CANCELED`, `DEAD`.\n- Every transition appends one immutable `job_events` row in same DB transaction as state update.\n\n## Worker Behavior\n- Concurrency model: asyncio tasks (`WORKER_CONCURRENCY`, default `4`) in one process.\n- Consume loop: `XREADGROUP BLOCK` on `jobs:{queue}`.\n- Reclaim loop: every `RECLAIM_INTERVAL_SEC` run `XPENDING` filter by idle > `VISIBILITY_TIMEOUT_SEC`, then `XCLAIM`.\n- Processing order:\n1. Receive stream message.\n2. Load job and atomically claim execution if runnable.\n3. Persist `RUNNING` and increment attempt count.\n4. Execute task with payload validation.\n5. Persist final/retry state + event.\n6. `XACK` only after successful durable persistence of final transition.\n- Retry policy:\n- Backoff `delay = min(cap, base * multiplier^(attempt-1)) + jitter`.\n- Jitter bounded by `jitter_ratio` (default `0.2`) and deterministic in tests via seeded RNG fixture.\n- Retryable failures re-enter `QUEUED` with `next_run_at = now + delay` and `enqueued=false`.\n- Exceeded attempts -> `DEAD` and publish entry to `jobs:dlq`.\n- Idempotency:\n- API dedupe for same key while non-terminal.\n- Worker checks `idempotency_locks` before side effects.\n- If key already `SUCCEEDED`, worker short-circuits and reuses stored result.\n- First executor acquires/owns lock; success stores result in lock row.\n- Cooperative cancellation:\n- `pipeline_demo` executes step-wise and checks `cancel_requested` between steps.\n- On cancellation request during running, transition to `CANCELED` at next checkpoint.\n\n## Scheduler Behavior\n- Delayed jobs:\n- Poll DB for `state=QUEUED`, `enqueued=false`, `next_run_at<=now`, `cancel_requested=false`.\n- Enqueue to Redis stream and set `enqueued=true`, `last_stream_id`.\n- If crash after enqueue before DB update, duplicate enqueue is tolerated; worker CAS logic prevents duplicate execution.\n- Recurring jobs:\n- Load schedules from YAML/JSON on startup.\n- Use croniter in UTC to compute planned ticks.\n- For each planned tick, insert `schedule_ticks` unique key `(schedule_id, planned_time)`.\n- Only successful insert creates job and increments `scheduler_enqueues_total{schedule_id}`.\n- Restart-safe dedupe comes from `schedule_ticks` uniqueness.\n\n## Observability\n- Structured JSON logs for API/worker/scheduler/CLI.\n- Mandatory correlation fields emitted when available: `job_id`, `stream_id`, `consumer`, `attempt`, `state`.\n- Prometheus metrics exposed at `/metrics` with required metrics:\n- `jobs_enqueued_total{type,queue}`\n- `jobs_started_total{type}`\n- `jobs_finished_total{type,state}`\n- `job_duration_seconds{type}` histogram\n- `queue_depth{queue}` gauge\n- `worker_reclaims_total`\n- `scheduler_enqueues_total{schedule_id}`\n\n## Docker, Startup, and Developer Commands\n- `docker-compose.yml` services:\n- `redis` with persistence.\n- `api` on `:8000`.\n- `worker` (default queue `default`).\n- `scheduler` loading `config/schedules.yaml`.\n- Shared volume for SQLite DB path `/app/data/mini_celery.db`.\n- Commands:\n- `make up` -> `docker compose up --build -d`\n- `make down` -> `docker compose down -v`\n- `make lint` -> `ruff check . && ruff format --check . && mypy mini_celery`\n- `make test` -> compose-based test run with Redis + pytest.\n- `make loadtest` -> CLI loadtest against running API.\n- Fallback for no `make`: `python scripts/dev.py <up|down|lint|test|loadtest>`.\n\n## Automated Tests\n- Unit tests:\n- State transition rules including invalid transitions rejection.\n- Backoff/jitter bounds and monotonic growth under cap.\n- API idempotency behavior for non-terminal duplicates.\n- Worker idempotency short-circuit when prior success exists.\n- Integration tests:\n- At-least-once with crash: kill worker mid-job, second worker reclaims from PEL and completes.\n- DLQ after max attempts: retryable task exceeds attempts, ends in `DEAD`, entry appears in `jobs:dlq`.\n- Delayed execution: job with future `run_at` never runs early; runs after due time.\n- Scheduler restart dedupe: same `(schedule_id, planned_time)` enqueued once across restart.\n- `make test` exits non-zero on any failing unit/integration test.\n\n## README Content Plan\n- Architecture diagram and service responsibilities.\n- Reliability walkthrough for worker crash, Redis restart, scheduler restart.\n- Quickstart with exact commands.\n- API examples for each endpoint.\n- How to add a new task type (Pydantic payload + registry + tests).\n- Design tradeoffs section.\n- Assumptions section.\n\n## Assumptions and Defaults\n- UTC-only timestamps; RFC3339 accepted/emitted with timezone.\n- Manual retry resets `attempts` to `0`, clears `last_error`, preserves `id`, `type`, `payload`, `idempotency_key`.\n- Default queue name is `default`.\n- Default `max_attempts=3`.\n- Worker concurrency uses asyncio tasks, not multiprocessing.\n- Backoff defaults: `base=1s`, `multiplier=2.0`, `cap=30s`, `jitter_ratio=0.2`.\n- Visibility timeout default `30s`; reclaim interval default `5s`.\n- SQLite runs in WAL mode with busy timeout to reduce write contention.\n- Extra endpoint `GET /jobs/{job_id}/events` is added to support CLI tailing cleanly.\n- Because this environment lacks local `docker` and `make`, implementation will include script fallbacks while preserving required `make` targets and Docker-first workflow.\n"
        }
      ]
    },

    {
      "type": "function_call",
      "name": "shell_command",
      "arguments": "{\"command\": \"@'\\n[build-system]\\nrequires = [\\\"hatchling>=1.24.0\\\"]\\nbuild-backend = \\\"hatchling.build\\\"\\n\\n[project]\\nname = \\\"mini-celery\\\"\\nversion = \\\"0.1.0\\\"\\ndescription = \\\"Local production-style job queue + scheduler using FastAPI, Redis Streams, and SQLite\\\"\\nreadme = \\\"README.md\\\"\\nrequires-python = \\\">=3.12\\\"\\nlicense = { text = \\\"MIT\\\" }\\nauthors = [{ name = \\\"mini-celery\\\" }]\\ndependencies = [\\n  \\\"fastapi>=0.115.0\\\",\\n  \\\"uvicorn[standard]>=0.31.0\\\",\\n  \\\"redis>=5.1.0\\\",\\n  \\\"SQLAlchemy>=2.0.36\\\",\\n  \\\"pydantic>=2.9.2\\\",\\n  \\\"pydantic-settings>=2.6.0\\\",\\n  \\\"httpx>=0.27.2\\\",\\n  \\\"prometheus-client>=0.21.0\\\",\\n  \\\"typer>=0.12.5\\\",\\n  \\\"croniter>=5.0.1\\\",\\n  \\\"PyYAML>=6.0.2\\\"\\n]\\n\\n[project.optional-dependencies]\\ndev = [\\n  \\\"pytest>=8.3.3\\\",\\n  \\\"pytest-asyncio>=0.24.0\\\",\\n  \\\"pytest-cov>=5.0.0\\\",\\n  \\\"ruff>=0.8.0\\\",\\n  \\\"mypy>=1.11.2\\\",\\n  \\\"types-PyYAML>=6.0.12.20240917\\\"\\n]\\n\\n[project.scripts]\\nmini-celery = \\\"mini_celery.cli:app\\\"\\n\\n[tool.pytest.ini_options]\\naddopts = \\\"-q\\\"\\nasyncio_mode = \\\"auto\\\"\\nmarkers = [\\n  \\\"integration: marks integration tests\\\"\\n]\\n\\n[tool.ruff]\\nline-length = 100\\ntarget-version = \\\"py312\\\"\\n\\n[tool.ruff.lint]\\nselect = [\\\"E\\\", \\\"F\\\", \\\"I\\\", \\\"UP\\\", \\\"B\\\", \\\"SIM\\\", \\\"RUF\\\"]\\n\\n[tool.ruff.format]\\nquote-style = \\\"double\\\"\\nindent-style = \\\"space\\\"\\n\\n[tool.mypy]\\npython_version = \\\"3.12\\\"\\nstrict = true\\nwarn_unused_ignores = true\\nwarn_redundant_casts = true\\nwarn_return_any = true\\ndisallow_any_generics = true\\nexclude = [\\\"tests/\\\"]\\n\\n[tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel]\\npackages = [\\\"mini_celery\\\"]\\n'@ | Set-Content -Path pyproject.toml\", \"workdir\": \"REDACTED\"}",
      "call_id": "REDACTED"
    },

      "arguments": "{\"command\": \"@'\\nservices:\\n  redis:\\n    image: redis:7-alpine\\n    command: [\\\"redis-server\\\", \\\"--appendonly\\\", \\\"yes\\\"]\\n    healthcheck:\\n      test: [\\\"CMD\\\", \\\"redis-cli\\\", \\\"ping\\\"]\\n      interval: 3s\\n      timeout: 3s\\n      retries: 10\\n    volumes:\\n      - redis-data:/data\\n\\n  api:\\n    build: .\\n    command: [\\\"uvicorn\\\", \\\"mini_celery.api.main:app\\\", \\\"--host\\\", \\\"0.0.0.0\\\", \\\"--port\\\", \\\"8000\\\"]\\n    environment:\\n      DATABASE_URL: sqlite:////app/data/mini_celery.db\\n      REDIS_URL: redis://redis:6379/0\\n      SCHEDULER_CONFIG_PATH: /app/config/schedules.yaml\\n      WORKER_QUEUES: default\\n    depends_on:\\n      redis:\\n        condition: service_healthy\\n    ports:\\n      - \\\"8000:8000\\\"\\n    volumes:\\n      - app-data:/app/data\\n\\n  worker:\\n    build: .\\n    command: [\\\"python\\\", \\\"-m\\\", \\\"mini_celery.worker.main\\\"]\\n    environment:\\n      DATABASE_URL: sqlite:////app/data/mini_celery.db\\n      REDIS_URL: redis://redis:6379/0\\n      WORKER_QUEUES: default\\n      WORKER_CONCURRENCY: 4\\n    depends_on:\\n      redis:\\n        condition: service_healthy\\n    volumes:\\n      - app-data:/app/data\\n\\n  scheduler:\\n    build: .\\n    command: [\\\"python\\\", \\\"-m\\\", \\\"mini_celery.scheduler.main\\\"]\\n    environment:\\n      DATABASE_URL: sqlite:////app/data/mini_celery.db\\n      REDIS_URL: redis://redis:6379/0\\n      SCHEDULER_CONFIG_PATH: /app/config/schedules.yaml\\n      WORKER_QUEUES: default\\n    depends_on:\\n      redis:\\n        condition: service_healthy\\n    volumes:\\n      - app-data:/app/data\\n\\nvolumes:\\n  app-data:\\n  redis-data:\\n'@ | Set-Content -Path docker-compose.yml\", \"workdir\": \"REDACTED\"}",

      "arguments": "{\"command\": \"@'\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\n\\nimport argparse\\nimport subprocess\\nimport sys\\n\\n\\ndef run(cmd: list[str]) -> int:\\n    print(\\\"$\\\", \\\" \\\".join(cmd), flush=True)\\n    return subprocess.call(cmd)\\n\\n\\ndef main() -> int:\\n    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=\\\"Fallback task runner when make is unavailable\\\")\\n    parser.add_argument(\\\"command\\\", choices=[\\\"up\\\", \\\"down\\\", \\\"lint\\\", \\\"test\\\", \\\"loadtest\\\"])\\n    args = parser.parse_args()\\n\\n    if args.command == \\\"up\\\":\\n        return run([\\\"docker\\\", \\\"compose\\\", \\\"up\\\", \\\"--build\\\", \\\"-d\\\"])\\n    if args.command == \\\"down\\\":\\n        return run([\\\"docker\\\", \\\"compose\\\", \\\"down\\\", \\\"-v\\\"])\\n    if args.command == \\\"lint\\\":\\n        steps = [\\n            [sys.executable, \\\"-m\\\", \\\"ruff\\\", \\\"check\\\", \\\".\\\"],\\n            [sys.executable, \\\"-m\\\", \\\"ruff\\\", \\\"format\\\", \\\"--check\\\", \\\".\\\"],\\n            [sys.executable, \\\"-m\\\", \\\"mypy\\\", \\\"mini_celery\\\"],\\n        ]\\n        for step in steps:\\n            code = run(step)\\n            if code:\\n                return code\\n        return 0\\n    if args.command == \\\"test\\\":\\n        code = run(\\n            [\\n                \\\"docker\\\",\\n                \\\"compose\\\",\\n                \\\"-f\\\",\\n                \\\"docker-compose.test.yml\\\",\\n                \\\"up\\\",\\n                \\\"--build\\\",\\n                \\\"--abort-on-container-exit\\\",\\n                \\\"--exit-code-from\\\",\\n                \\\"test\\\",\\n            ]\\n        )\\n        down_code = run([\\\"docker\\\", \\\"compose\\\", \\\"-f\\\", \\\"docker-compose.test.yml\\\", \\\"down\\\", \\\"-v\\\"])\\n        return code or down_code\\n    if args.command == \\\"loadtest\\\":\\n        return run(\\n            [\\n                sys.executable,\\n                \\\"-m\\\",\\n                \\\"mini_celery.cli\\\",\\n                \\\"loadtest\\\",\\n                \\\"--total\\\",\\n                \\\"100\\\",\\n                \\\"--concurrency\\\",\\n                \\\"10\\\",\\n                \\\"--task-type\\\",\\n                \\\"sleep\\\",\\n                \\\"--payload\\\",\\n                '{\\\"seconds\\\":0.05}',\\n            ]\\n        )\\n\\n    return 1\\n\\n\\nif __name__ == \\\"__main__\\\":\\n    raise SystemExit(main())\\n'@ | Set-Content -Path scripts\\\\dev.py\",       "type": "function_call",
      "name": "shell_command",
      "arguments": "{\"command\": \"@'\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\n\\nfrom functools import lru_cache\\n\\nfrom pydantic import Field\\nfrom pydantic_settings import BaseSettings, SettingsConfigDict\\n\\n\\nclass Settings(BaseSettings):\\n    model_config = SettingsConfigDict(env_file=\\\".env\\\", extra=\\\"ignore\\\")\\n\\n    app_name: str = \\\"mini-celery\\\"\\n    log_level: str = \\\"INFO\\\"\\n\\n    database_url: str = \\\"sqlite:///REDACTED\\\"\\n    redis_url: str = \\\"redis://localhost:6379/0\\\"\\n\\n    stream_prefix: str = \\\"jobs\\\"\\n    consumer_group: str = \\\"workers\\\"\\n    default_queue: str = \\\"default\\\"\\n    worker_queues: str = \\\"default\\\"\\n\\n    worker_concurrency: int = 4\\n    visibility_timeout_sec: int = 30\\n    reclaim_interval_sec: int = 5\\n\\n    scheduler_poll_interval_sec: float = 1.0\\n    scheduler_config_path: str = \\\"config/schedules.yaml\\\"\\n\\n    retry_base_delay_sec: float = 1.0\\n    retry_multiplier: float = 2.0\\n    retry_cap_delay_sec: float = 30.0\\n    retry_jitter_ratio: float = 0.2\\n\\n    api_url: str = Field(default=\\\"http://localhost:8000\\\", alias=\\\"API_URL\\\")\\n\\n    def stream_name(self, queue: str) -> str:\\n        return f\\\"{self.stream_prefix}:{queue}\\\"\\n\\n    @property\\n    def dlq_stream_name(self) -> str:\\n        return f\\\"{self.stream_prefix}:dlq\\\"\\n\\n    @property\\n    def queues(self) -> list[str]:\\n        return [q.strip() for q in self.worker_queues.split(\\\",\\\") if q.strip()]\\n\\n\\n@lru_cache(maxsize=1)\\ndef get_settings() -> Settings:\\n    return Settings()\\n\\n\\ndef reset_settings_cache() -> None:\\n    get_settings.cache_clear()\\n'@ | Set-Content -Path mini_celery\\\\config.py\", \"workdir\": \"REDACTED\"}",
      "call_id": "REDACTED"

      "type": "function_call",
      "name": "shell_command",
      "arguments": "{\"command\": \"@'\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\n\\nimport json\\nimport logging\\nfrom datetime import UTC, datetime\\nfrom typing import Any\\n\\n\\nclass JsonLogFormatter(logging.Formatter):\\n    def format(self, record: logging.LogRecord) -> str:\\n        base: dict[str, Any] = {\\n            \\\"ts\\\": datetime.now(UTC).isoformat(),\\n            \\\"level\\\": record.levelname,\\n            \\\"logger\\\": record.name,\\n            \\\"message\\\": record.getMessage(),\\n        }\\n\\n        if record.exc_info:\\n            base[\\\"exc_info\\\"] = self.formatException(record.exc_info)\\n\\n        standard = {\\n            \\\"name\\\",\\n            \\\"msg\\\",\\n            \\\"args\\\",\\n            \\\"levelname\\\",\\n            \\\"levelno\\\",\\n            \\\"pathname\\\",\\n            \\\"filename\\\",\\n            \\\"module\\\",\\n            \\\"exc_info\\\",\\n            \\\"exc_text\\\",\\n            \\\"stack_info\\\",\\n            \\\"lineno\\\",\\n            \\\"funcName\\\",\\n            \\\"created\\\",\\n            \\\"msecs\\\",\\n            \\\"relativeCreated\\\",\\n            \\\"thread\\\",\\n            \\\"threadName\\\",\\n            \\\"processName\\\",\\n            \\\"process\\\",\\n            \\\"taskName\\\",\\n        }\\n        for key, value in record.__dict__.items():\\n            if key not in standard and not key.startswith(\\\"_\\\"):\\n                base[key] = value\\n\\n        return json.dumps(base, default=str)\\n\\n\\ndef setup_logging(level: str = \\\"INFO\\\") -> None:\\n    root = logging.getLogger()\\n    root.setLevel(level.upper())\\n    handler = logging.StreamHandler()\\n    handler.setFormatter(JsonLogFormatter())\\n    root.handlers.clear()\\n    root.addHandler(handler)\\n'@ | Set-Content -Path mini_celery\\\\logging.py\", \"workdir\": \"REDACTED\"}",
      "call_id": "REDACTED"
    },

      "arguments": "{\"command\": \"@'\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\n\\nfrom prometheus_client import CONTENT_TYPE_LATEST, Counter, Gauge, Histogram, generate_latest\\n\\njobs_enqueued_total = Counter(\\\"jobs_enqueued_total\\\", \\\"Jobs enqueued\\\", [\\\"type\\\", \\\"queue\\\"])\\njobs_started_total = Counter(\\\"jobs_started_total\\\", \\\"Jobs started\\\", [\\\"type\\\"])\\njobs_finished_total = Counter(\\\"jobs_finished_total\\\", \\\"Jobs finished\\\", [\\\"type\\\", \\\"state\\\"])\\njob_duration_seconds = Histogram(\\\"job_duration_seconds\\\", \\\"Job execution duration\\\", [\\\"type\\\"])\\nqueue_depth = Gauge(\\\"queue_depth\\\", \\\"Queue depth\\\", [\\\"queue\\\"])\\nworker_reclaims_total = Counter(\\\"worker_reclaims_total\\\", \\\"Reclaimed pending messages\\\")\\nscheduler_enqueues_total = Counter(\\n    \\\"scheduler_enqueues_total\\\", \\\"Scheduler tick enqueues\\\", [\\\"schedule_id\\\"]\\n)\\n\\n\\ndef metrics_payload() -> bytes:\\n    return generate_latest()\\n\\n\\ndef metrics_content_type() -> str:\\n    return CONTENT_TYPE_LATEST\\n'@ | Set-Content -Path mini_celery\\\\metrics.py\", \"workdir\": \"REDACTED\"}",

      "type": "function_call",
      "name": "shell_command",
      "arguments": "{\"command\": \"@'\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\n\\nfrom datetime import UTC, datetime\\nfrom enum import Enum\\nfrom typing import Any\\nfrom uuid import uuid4\\n\\nfrom sqlalchemy import Boolean, DateTime, Integer, JSON, String, Text, UniqueConstraint\\nfrom sqlalchemy.orm import DeclarativeBase, Mapped, mapped_column\\n\\n\\nclass Base(DeclarativeBase):\\n    pass\\n\\n\\nclass JobState(str, Enum):\\n    QUEUED = \\\"QUEUED\\\"\\n    RUNNING = \\\"RUNNING\\\"\\n    SUCCEEDED = \\\"SUCCEEDED\\\"\\n    FAILED = \\\"FAILED\\\"\\n    CANCELED = \\\"CANCELED\\\"\\n    DEAD = \\\"DEAD\\\"\\n\\n\\nTERMINAL_STATES: set[JobState] = {\\n    JobState.SUCCEEDED,\\n    JobState.FAILED,\\n    JobState.CANCELED,\\n    JobState.DEAD,\\n}\\n\\n\\nclass IdempotencyStatus(str, Enum):\\n    IN_PROGRESS = \\\"IN_PROGRESS\\\"\\n    SUCCEEDED = \\\"SUCCEEDED\\\"\\n\\n\\ndef utc_now() -> datetime:\\n    return datetime.now(UTC)\\n\\n\\nclass Job(Base):\\n    __tablename__ = \\\"jobs\\\"\\n\\n    id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(36), primary_key=True, default=lambda: str(uuid4()))\\n    type: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(100), index=True)\\n    payload: Mapped[dict[str, Any]] = mapped_column(JSON)\\n    state: Mapped[JobState] = mapped_column(String(20), index=True)\\n    created_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(DateTime(timezone=True), default=utc_now, index=True)\\n    updated_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(DateTime(timezone=True), default=utc_now, onupdate=utc_now)\\n\\n    idempotency_key: Mapped[str | None] = mapped_column(String(200), nullable=True, index=True)\\n    attempts: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(Integer, default=0)\\n    max_attempts: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(Integer, default=3)\\n\\n    next_run_at: Mapped[datetime | None] = mapped_column(DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=True, index=True)\\n    schedule: Mapped[str | None] = mapped_column(String(200), nullable=True)\\n\\n    last_error: Mapped[str | None] = mapped_column(Text, nullable=True)\\n    result: Mapped[dict[str, Any] | None] = mapped_column(JSON, nullable=True)\\n\\n    queue: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(100), default=\\\"default\\\", index=True)\\n    cancel_requested: Mapped[bool] = mapped_column(Boolean, default=False)\\n    lease_expires_at: Mapped[datetime | None] = mapped_column(DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=True)\\n    last_stream_id: Mapped[str | None] = mapped_column(String(64), nullable=True)\\n    enqueued:       "type": "function_call",
      "name": "shell_command",
      "arguments": "{\"command\": \"@'\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\n\\nfrom contextlib import contextmanager\\nfrom typing import Iterator\\n\\nfrom sqlalchemy import create_engine, event, text\\nfrom sqlalchemy.engine import Engine\\nfrom sqlalchemy.orm import Session, sessionmaker\\n\\nfrom mini_celery.config import Settings, get_settings\\nfrom mini_celery.models import Base\\n\\n\\nclass Database:\\n    def __init__(self, database_url: str):\\n        connect_args: dict[str, object] = {}\\n        if database_url.startswith(\\\"sqlite\\\"):\\n            connect_args = {\\\"check_same_thread\\\": False}\\n\\n        self.engine = create_engine(database_url, connect_args=connect_args, future=True)\\n        self.SessionLocal = sessionmaker(\\n            bind=self.engine,\\n            class_=Session,\\n            autocommit=False,\\n            autoflush=False,\\n            expire_on_commit=False,\\n        )\\n\\n        if database_url.startswith(\\\"sqlite\\\"):\\n\\n            @event.listens_for(self.engine, \\\"connect\\\")\\n            def _set_sqlite_pragma(dbapi_connection: object, _: object) -> None:\\n                cursor = dbapi_connection.cursor()\\n                cursor.execute(\\\"PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL;\\\")\\n                cursor.execute(\\\"PRAGMA busy_timeout=5000;\\\")\\n                cursor.execute(\\\"PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON;\\\")\\n                cursor.close()\\n\\n    def init(self) -> None:\\n        Base.metadata.create_all(bind=self.engine)\\n\\n    @contextmanager\\n    def session(self) -> Iterator[Session]:\\n        session = self.SessionLocal()\\n        try:\\n            yield session\\n            session.commit()\\n        except Exception:\\n            session.rollback()\\n            raise\\n        finally:\\n            session.close()\\n\\n    def check(self) -> bool:\\n        with self.session() as session:\\n            session.execute(text(\\\"SELECT 1\\\"))\\n        return True\\n\\n\\n_default_settings: Settings = get_settings()\\ndefault_db = Database(_default_settings.database_url)\\n\\n\\ndef get_db() -> Database:\\n    return default_db\\n\\n\\ndef get_engine() -> Engine:\\n    return default_db.engine\\n'@ | Set-Content -Path mini_celery\\\\db.py\", \"workdir\": \"REDACTED\"}",

      "type": "function_call",
      "name": "shell_command",
      "arguments": "{\"command\": \"@'\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\n\\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\\n\\nfrom mini_celery.models import JobState\\n\\n\\nclass InvalidTransition(ValueError):\\n    pass\\n\\n\\nALLOWED_TRANSITIONS: dict[JobState, set[JobState]] = {\\n    JobState.QUEUED: {JobState.RUNNING, JobState.CANCELED},\\n    JobState.RUNNING: {\\n        JobState.SUCCEEDED,\\n        JobState.FAILED,\\n        JobState.CANCELED,\\n        JobState.DEAD,\\n        JobState.QUEUED,\\n    },\\n    JobState.FAILED: {JobState.QUEUED},\\n    JobState.DEAD: {JobState.QUEUED},\\n    JobState.SUCCEEDED: set(),\\n    JobState.CANCELED: set(),\\n}\\n\\n\\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\\nclass Transition:\\n    old: JobState\\n    new: JobState\\n\\n\\ndef can_transition(old: JobState, new: JobState) -> bool:\\n    return new in ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS.get(old, set())\\n\\n\\ndef assert_transition(old: JobState, new: JobState) -> None:\\n    if not can_transition(old, new):\\n        raise InvalidTransition(f\\\"Invalid transition: {old} -> {new}\\\")\\n'@ | Set-Content -Path mini_celery\\\\state_machine.py\", \"workdir\": \"REDACTED\"}",

      "type": "function_call",
      "name": "shell_command",
      "arguments": "{\"command\": \"@'\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\n\\nimport random\\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\\n\\n\\n@dataclass(slots=True)\\nclass BackoffPolicy:\\n    base_delay_sec: float = 1.0\\n    multiplier: float = 2.0\\n    cap_delay_sec: float = 30.0\\n    jitter_ratio: float = 0.2\\n\\n    def compute_delay(self, attempt: int, rng: random.Random | None = None) -> float:\\n        if attempt < 1:\\n            raise ValueError(\\\"attempt must be >= 1\\\")\\n        effective_rng = rng or random.Random()\\n\\n        base = min(self.cap_delay_sec, self.base_delay_sec * (self.multiplier ** (attempt - 1)))\\n        jitter_span = base * self.jitter_ratio\\n        jitter = (effective_rng.random() * 2.0 - 1.0) * jitter_span\\n        delay = max(0.0, base + jitter)\\n        return delay\\n'@ | Set-Content -Path mini_celery\\\\backoff.py\", \"workdir\": \"REDACTED\"}",

      "arguments": "{\"command\": \"@'\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\n\\nfrom datetime import datetime\\nfrom typing import Any\\nfrom uuid import UUID\\n\\nfrom pydantic import BaseModel, ConfigDict, Field\\n\\nfrom mini_celery.models import Job, JobEvent, JobState\\n\\n\\nclass CreateJobRequest(BaseModel):\\n    type: str\\n    payload: dict[str, Any]\\n    queue: str = \\\"default\\\"\\n    max_attempts: int = Field(default=3, ge=1, le=100)\\n    run_at: datetime | None = None\\n    idempotency_key: str | None = Field(default=None, max_length=200)\\n\\n\\nclass CreateJobResponse(BaseModel):\\n    job_id: UUID\\n\\n\\nclass JobResponse(BaseModel):\\n    model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)\\n\\n    id: UUID\\n    type: str\\n    payload: dict[str, Any]\\n    state: JobState\\n    created_at: datetime\\n    updated_at: datetime\\n    idempotency_key: str | None = None\\n    attempts: int\\n    max_attempts: int\\n    next_run_at: datetime | None = None\\n    schedule: str | None = None\\n    last_error: str | None = None\\n    result: dict[str, Any] | None = None\\n    queue: str\\n    cancel_requested: bool\\n    lease_expires_at: datetime | None = None\\n    last_stream_id: str | None = None\\n    enqueued: bool\\n    version: int\\n\\n\\nclass JobEventResponse(BaseModel):\\n    model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)\\n\\n    id: int\\n    job_id: UUID\\n    ts: datetime\\n    old_state: str | None\\n    new_state: str\\n    message: str\\n    metadata: dict[str, Any] | None = None\\n\\n\\nclass JobsListResponse(BaseModel):\\n    items: list[JobResponse]\\n    next_cursor: str | None = None\\n\\n\\nclass JobEventsListResponse(BaseModel):\\n    items: list[JobEventResponse]\\n    next_cursor_ts: datetime | None = None\\n\\n\\nclass ActionJobResponse(BaseModel):\\n    job: JobResponse\\n\\n\\ndef job_to_response(job: Job) -> JobResponse:\\n    return JobResponse.model_validate(job)\\n\\n\\ndef jobs_to_response(jobs: list[Job]) -> list[JobResponse]:\\n    return [job_to_response(job) for job in jobs]\\n\\n\\ndef events_to_response(events: list[JobEvent]) -> list[JobEventResponse]:\\n    return [JobEventResponse.model_validate(event) for event in events]\\n'@ | Set-Content -Path mini_celery\\\\schemas.py\", \"workdir\": \"REDACTED\"}",

      "arguments": "{\"command\": \"@'\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\n\\nfrom collections.abc import Mapping\\nfrom typing import Protocol\\n\\n\\nclass BrokerMessage(Protocol):\\n    stream_id: str\\n    queue: str\\n    fields: dict[str, str]\\n\\n\\nclass Broker(Protocol):\\n    async def ensure_consumer_group(self, queue: str) -> None: ..REDACTED    async def enqueue(self, queue: str, fields: Mapping[str, str]) -> str: ..REDACTED    async def read_group(\\n        self,\\n        queue: str,\\n        consumer: str,\\n        count: int,\\n        block_ms: int,\\n    ) -> list[tuple[str, dict[str, str]]]: ..REDACTED    async def ack(self, queue: str, stream_id: str) -> int: ..REDACTED    async def claim_stale(\\n        self,\\n        queue: str,\\n        consumer: str,\\n        min_idle_ms: int,\\n        count: int,\\n    ) -> list[tuple[str, dict[str, str]]]: ..REDACTED    async def queue_depth(self, queue: str) -> int: ..REDACTED    async def publish_dlq(self, fields: Mapping[str, str]) -> str: ..REDACTED    async def ping(self) -> bool: ..REDACTED    async def close(self) -> None: ...\\n'@ | Set-Content -Path mini_celery\\\\broker\\\\base.py\", \"workdir\": \"REDACTED\"}",
