"output_text"}], "phase": "commentary", "role": "assistant", "type": "message"}, {"content": [{"text": "<proposed_plan>\n# Mini Celery (Python 3.12) — Decision-Complete Build Plan\n\n## Summary\n- Build a greenfield repository named `mini_celery` with four runnables: FastAPI API, Redis Streams worker, scheduler, and CLI.\n- Use Redis Streams + consumer groups for at-least-once delivery and reclaim via `XPENDING` + `XCLAIM`.\n- Persist authoritative job state and transition history in SQLite via SQLAlchemy 2.0.\n- Provide deterministic behavior via explicit UTC timestamps, injectable clock, bounded jitter, strict state machine, and structured error paths.\n- Ship Docker Compose startup (`redis`, `api`, `worker`, `scheduler`) plus `make` targets and script fallbacks.\n- Include unit + integration tests that directly cover all required reliability scenarios.\n\n## Repository Layout\n- `pyproject.toml` with dependencies, ruff, pytest, mypy config.\n- `Dockerfile` single image used by API/worker/scheduler/cli.\n- `docker-compose.yml` for local runtime.\n- `docker-compose.test.yml` for isolated integration test execution.\n- `Makefile` with `test`, `lint`, `up`, `down`, `loadtest`.\n- `scripts/dev.py` fallback task runner for environments without `make`.\n- `README.md` with architecture, quickstart, failure scenarios, tradeoffs, assumptions, extensibility.\n- `config/schedules.yaml` recurring schedule examples.\n- `mini_celery/__init__.py`\n- `mini_celery/config.py` env settings.\n- `mini_celery/logging.py` JSON logging setup.\n- `mini_celery/metrics.py` Prometheus counters/gauges/histograms.\n- `mini_celery/db.py` engine/session/init.\n- `mini_celery/models.py` SQLAlchemy models and enums.\n- `mini_celery/schemas.py` Pydantic request/response models.\n- `mini_celery/state_machine.py` allowed transitions + validation.\n- `mini_celery/repository.py` DB operations (jobs, events, idempotency, schedule ticks).\n- `mini_celery/broker/base.py` broker protocol.\n- `mini_celery/broker/redis_streams.py` stream/group operations, reclaim, dlq.\n- `mini_celery/tasks/base.py` task protocol + registry.\n- `mini_celery/tasks/types.py` `sleep`, `http_get`, `cpu_burn`, `fail_then_succeed`, `pipeline_demo`.\n- `mini_celery/worker/main.py` worker service.\n- `mini_celery/scheduler/main.py` delayed + recurring scheduler service.\n- `mini_celery/api/main.py` FastAPI app wiring.\n- `mini_celery/api/routes_jobs.py` jobs/admin/events endpoints.\n- `mini_celery/api/routes_health.py` `/healthz`.\n- `mini_celery/cli.py` submit/status/tail/loadtest.\n- `tests/unit/...`\n- `tests/integration/...`\n- `tests/conftest.py`\n\n## Public APIs, Interfaces, and Types\n- HTTP API (required + one additive endpoint):\n1. `POST /jobs` exact request contract; returns `{ \"job_id\": \"<uuid>\" }`.\n2. `GET /jobs/{job_id}` returns full job record.\n3. `POST /jobs/{job_id}/cancel` supports queued/delayed cancellation and running cancel request.\n4. `POST /jobs/{job_id}/retry` allowed only for `FAILED`/`DEAD`; resets `attempts=0` and requeues.\n5. `GET /jobs?state=&type=&limit=&cursor=` cursor pagination.\n6. `GET /metrics` Prometheus exposition.\n7. `GET /healthz` liveness + Redis + DB checks.\n8. `GET /jobs/{job_id}/events?cursor_ts=` additive endpoint for CLI tail polling.\n- Redis stream message schema:\n- Fields: `job_id`, `type`, `queue`, `enqueued_at`, `dispatch_token`.\n- Streams: `jobs:{queue}` and DLQ stream `jobs:dlq`.\n- Group: `workers`.\n- Scheduler config schema (`config/schedules.yaml`):\n- `id`, `cron`, `queue`, `type`, `payload`, `max_attempts`, `enabled`.\n- CLI commands:\n- `submit`, `status`, `list`, `tail-events`, `loadtest`.\n\n## Data Model\n- Required `jobs` table with all requested columns.\n- Required `job_events` append-only table with requested columns.\n- Added columns in `jobs` for reliability:\n- `queue` (string), `cancel_requested` (bool), `lease_expires_at` (datetime nullable), `last_stream_id` (string nullable), `enqueued` (bool), `version` (int optimistic concurrency).\n- Added table `idempotency_locks`:\n- `key` (PK), `owner_job_id`, `status` (`IN_PROGRESS|SUCCEEDED`), `result` JSON, `updated_at`.\n- Added table `schedule_ticks`:\n- `schedule_id`, `planned_time`, `job_id`, `created_at`; unique `(schedule_id, planned_time)`.\n\n## State Machine and Core Semantics\n- States: `QUEUED`, `RUNNING`, `SUCCEEDED`, `FAILED`, `CANCELED`, `DEAD`.\n- Allowed transitions:\n- `QUEUED -> RUNNING|CANCELED`\n- `RUNNING -> SUCCEEDED|FAILED|CANCELED|DEAD|QUEUED` (queued only for retryable failures/backoff)\n- `FAILED -> QUEUED` (manual retry)\n- `DEAD -> QUEUED` (manual retry)\n- Terminal states: `SUCCEEDED`, `FAILED`, `CANCELED`, `DEAD`.\n- Every transition appends one immutable `job_events` row in same DB transaction as state update.\n\n## Worker Behavior\n- Concurrency model: asyncio tasks (`WORKER_CONCURRENCY`, default `4`) in one process.\n- Consume loop: `XREADGROUP BLOCK` on `jobs:{queue}`.\n- Reclaim loop: every `RECLAIM_INTERVAL_SEC` run `XPENDING` filter by idle > `VISIBILITY_TIMEOUT_SEC`, then `XCLAIM`.\n- Processing order:\n1. Receive stream message.\n2. Load job and atomically claim execution if runnable.\n3. Persist `RUNNING` and increment attempt count.\n4. Execute task with payload validation.\n5. Persist final/retry state + event.\n6. `XACK` only after successful durable persistence of final transition.\n- Retry policy:\n- Backoff `delay = min(cap, base * multiplier^(attempt-1)) + jitter`.\n- Jitter bounded by `jitter_ratio` (default `0.2`) and deterministic in tests via seeded RNG fixture.\n- Retryable failures re-enter `QUEUED` with `next_run_at = now + delay` and `enqueued=false`.\n- Exceeded attempts -> `DEAD` and publish entry to `jobs:dlq`.\n- Idempotency:\n- API dedupe for same key while non-terminal.\n- Worker checks `idempotency_locks` before side effects.\n- If key already `SUCCEEDED`, worker short-circuits and reuses stored result.\n- First executor acquires/owns lock; success stores result in lock row.\n- Cooperative cancellation:\n- `pipeline_demo` executes step-wise and checks `cancel_requested` between steps.\n- On cancellation request during running, transition to `CANCELED` at next checkpoint.\n\n## Scheduler Behavior\n- Delayed jobs:\n- Poll DB for `state=QUEUED`, `enqueued=false`, `next_run_at<=now`, `cancel_requested=false`.\n- Enqueue to Redis stream and set `enqueued=true`, `last_stream_id`.\n- If crash after enqueue before DB update, duplicate enqueue is tolerated; worker CAS logic prevents duplicate execution.\n- Recurring jobs:\n- Load schedules from YAML/JSON on startup.\n- Use croniter in UTC to compute planned ticks.\n- For each planned tick, insert `schedule_ticks` unique key `(schedule_id, planned_time)`.\n- Only successful insert creates job and increments `scheduler_enqueues_total{schedule_id}`.\n- Restart-safe dedupe comes from `schedule_ticks` uniqueness.\n\n## Observability\n- Structured JSON logs for API/worker/scheduler/CLI.\n- Mandatory correlation fields emitted when available: `job_id`, `stream_id`, `consumer`, `attempt`, `state`.\n- Prometheus metrics exposed at `/metrics` with required metrics:\n- `jobs_enqueued_total{type,queue}`\n- `jobs_started_total{type}`\n- `jobs_finished_total{type,state}`\n- `job_duration_seconds{type}` histogram\n- `queue_depth{queue}` gauge\n- `worker_reclaims_total`\n- `scheduler_enqueues_total{schedule_id}`\n\n## Docker, Startup, and Developer Commands\n- `docker-compose.yml` services:\n- `redis` with persistence.\n- `api` on `:8000`.\n- `worker` (default queue `default`).\n- `scheduler` loading `config/schedules.yaml`.\n- Shared volume for SQLite DB path `/app/data/mini_celery.db`.\n- Commands:\n- `make up` -> `docker compose up --build -d`\n- `make down` -> `docker compose down -v`\n- `make lint` -> `ruff check . && ruff format --check . && mypy mini_celery`\n- `make test` -> compose-based test run with Redis + pytest.\n- `make loadtest` -> CLI loadtest against running API.\n- Fallback for no `make`: `python scripts/dev.py <up|down|lint|test|loadtest>`.\n\n## Automated Tests\n- Unit tests:\n- State transition rules including invalid transitions rejection.\n- Backoff/jitter bounds and monotonic growth under cap.\n- API idempotency behavior for non-terminal duplicates.\n- Worker idempotency short-circuit when prior success exists.\n- Integration tests:\n- At-least-once with crash: kill worker mid-job, second worker reclaims from PEL and completes.\n- DLQ after max attempts: retryable task exceeds attempts, ends in `DEAD`, entry appears in `jobs:dlq`.\n- Delayed execution: job with future `run_at` never runs early; runs after due time.\n- Scheduler restart dedupe: same `(schedule_id, planned_time)` enqueued once across restart.\n- `make test` exits non-zero on any failing unit/integration test.\n\n## README Content Plan\n- Architecture diagram and service responsibilities.\n- Reliability walkthrough for worker crash, Redis restart, scheduler restart.\n- Quickstart with exact commands.\n- API examples for each endpoint.\n- How to add a new task type (Pydantic payload + registry + tests).\n- Design tradeoffs section.\n- Assumptions section.\n\n## Assumptions and Defaults\n- UTC-only timestamps; RFC3339 accepted/emitted with timezone.\n- Manual retry resets `attempts` to `0`, clears `last_error`, preserves `id`, `type`, `payload`, `idempotency_key`.\n- Default queue name is `default`.\n- Default `max_attempts=3`.\n- Worker concurrency uses asyncio tasks, not multiprocessing.\n- Backoff defaults: `base=1s`, `multiplier=2.0`, `cap=30s`, `jitter_ratio=0.2`.\n- Visibility timeout default `30s`; reclaim interval default `5s`.\n- SQLite runs in WAL mode with busy timeout to reduce write contention.\n- Extra endpoint `GET /jobs/{job_id}/events` is added to support CLI tailing cleanly.\n- Because this environment lacks local `docker` and `make`, implementation will include script fallbacks while preserving required `make` targets and Docker-first workflow.\n</proposed_plan>", "type": "output_text"}], "phase": "final_answer", "role": "assistant", "type": "message"}, {"content": [{"text": "<collaboration_mode># Collaboration Mode: Default\r\n\r\nYou are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\r\n\r\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\r\n\r\n## request_user_input availability\r\n\r\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\r\n\r\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\r\n</collaboration_mode>", "type": "input_text"}], "role": "developer", "type": "message"}, {"content": [{"text": "PLEASE IMPLEMENT THIS PLAN:\n# Mini Celery (Python 3.12) — Decision-Complete Build Plan\n\n## Summary\n- Build a greenfield repository named `mini_celery` with four runnables: FastAPI API, Redis Streams worker, scheduler, and CLI.\n- Use Redis Streams + consumer groups for at-least-once delivery and reclaim via `XPENDING` + `XCLAIM`.\n- Persist authoritative job state and transition history in SQLite via SQLAlchemy 2.0.\n- Provide deterministic behavior via explicit UTC timestamps, injectable clock, bounded jitter, strict state machine, and structured error paths.\n- Ship Docker Compose startup (`redis`, `api`, `worker`, `scheduler`) plus `make` targets and script fallbacks.\n- Include unit + integration tests that directly cover all required reliability scenarios.\n\n## Repository Layout\n- `pyproject.toml` with dependencies, ruff, pytest, mypy config.\n- `Dockerfile` single image used by API/worker/scheduler/cli.\n- `docker-compose.yml` for local runtime.\n- `docker-compose.test.yml` for isolated integration test execution.\n- `Makefile` with `test`, `lint`, `up`, `down`, `loadtest`.\n- `scripts/dev.py` fallback task runner for environments without `make`.\n- `README.md` with architecture, quickstart, failure scenarios, tradeoffs, assumptions, extensibility.\n- `config/schedules.yaml` recurring schedule examples.\n- `mini_celery/__init__.py`\n- `mini_celery/config.py` env settings.\n- `mini_celery/logging.py` JSON logging setup.\n- `mini_celery/metrics.py` Prometheus counters/gauges/histograms.\n- `mini_celery/db.py` engine/session/init.\n- `mini_celery/models.py` SQLAlchemy models and enums.\n- `mini_celery/schemas.py` Pydantic request/response models.\n- `mini_celery/state_machine.py` allowed transitions + validation.\n- `mini_celery/repository.py` DB operations (jobs, events, idempotency, schedule ticks).\n- `mini_celery/broker/base.py` broker protocol.\n- `mini_celery/broker/redis_streams.py` stream/group operations, reclaim, dlq.\n- `mini_celery/tasks/base.py` task protocol + registry.\n- `mini_celery/tasks/types.py` `sleep`, `http_get`, `cpu_burn`, `fail_then_succeed`, `pipeline_demo`.\n- `mini_celery/worker/main.py` worker service.\n- `mini_celery/scheduler/main.py` delayed + recurring scheduler service.\n- `mini_celery/api/main.py` FastAPI app wiring.\n- `mini_celery/api/routes_jobs.py` jobs/admin/events endpoints.\n- `mini_celery/api/routes_health.py` `/healthz`.\n- `mini_celery/cli.py` submit/status/tail/loadtest.\n- `tests/unit/...`\n- `tests/integration/...`\n- `tests/conftest.py`\n\n## Public APIs, Interfaces, and Types\n- HTTP API (required + one additive endpoint):\n1. `POST /jobs` exact request contract; returns `{ \"job_id\": \"<uuid>\" }`.\n2. `GET /jobs/{job_id}` returns full job record.\n3. `POST /jobs/{job_id}/cancel` supports queued/delayed cancellation and running cancel request.\n4. `POST /jobs/{job_id}/retry` allowed only for `FAILED`/`DEAD`; resets `attempts=0` and requeues.\n5. `GET /jobs?state=&type=&limit=&cursor=` cursor pagination.\n6. `GET /metrics` Prometheus exposition.\n7. `GET /healthz` liveness + Redis + DB checks.\n8. `GET /jobs/{job_id}/events?cursor_ts=` additive endpoint for CLI tail polling.\n- Redis stream message schema:\n- Fields: `job_id`, `type`, `queue`, `enqueued_at`, `dispatch_token`.\n- Streams: `jobs:{queue}` and DLQ stream `jobs:dlq`.\n- Group: `workers`.\n- Scheduler config schema (`config/schedules.yaml`):\n- `id`, `cron`, `queue`, `type`, `payload`, `max_attempts`, `enabled`.\n- CLI commands:\n- `submit`, `status`, `list`, `tail-events`, `loadtest`.\n\n## Data Model\n- Required `jobs` table with all requested columns.\n- Required `job_events` append-only table with requested columns.\n- Added columns in `jobs` for reliability:\n- `queue` (string), `cancel_requested` (bool), `lease_expires_at` (datetime nullable), `last_stream_id` (string nullable), `enqueued` (bool), `version` (int optimistic concurrency).\n- Added table `idempotency_locks`:\n- `key` (PK), `owner_job_id`, `status` (`IN_PROGRESS|SUCCEEDED`), `result` JSON, `updated_at`.\n- Added table `schedule_ticks`:\n- `schedule_id`, `planned_time`, `job_id`, `created_at`; unique `(schedule_id, planned_time)`.\n\n## State Machine and Core Semantics\n- States: `QUEUED`, `RUNNING`, `SUCCEEDED`, `FAILED`, `CANCELED`, `DEAD`.\n- Allowed transitions:\n- `QUEUED -> RUNNING|CANCELED`\n- `RUNNING -> SUCCEEDED|FAILED|CANCELED|DEAD|QUEUED` (queued only for retryable failures/backoff)\n- `FAILED -> QUEUED` (manual retry)\n- `DEAD -> QUEUED` (manual retry)\n- Terminal states: `SUCCEEDED`, `FAILED`, `CANCELED`, `DEAD`.\n- Every transition appends one immutable `job_events` row in same DB transaction as state update.\n\n## Worker Behavior\n- Concurrency model: asyncio tasks (`WORKER_CONCURRENCY`, default `4`) in one process.\n- Consume loop: `XREADGROUP BLOCK` on `jobs:{queue}`.\n- Reclaim loop: every `RECLAIM_INTERVAL_SEC` run `XPENDING` filter by idle > `VISIBILITY_TIMEOUT_SEC`, then `XCLAIM`.\n- Processing order:\n1. Receive stream message.\n2. Load job and atomically claim execution if runnable.\n3. Persist `RUNNING` and increment attempt count.\n4. Execute task with payload validation.\n5. Persist final/retry state + event.\n6. `XACK` only after successful durable persistence of final transition.\n- Retry policy:\n- Backoff `delay = min(cap, base * multiplier^(attempt-1)) + jitter`.\n- Jitter bounded by `jitter_ratio` (default `0.2`) and deterministic in tests via seeded RNG fixture.\n- Retryable failures re-enter `QUEUED` with `next_run_at = now + delay` and `enqueued=false`.\n- Exceeded attempts -> `DEAD` and publish entry to `jobs:dlq`.\n- Idempotency:\n- API dedupe for same key while non-terminal.\n- Worker checks `idempotency_locks` before side effects.\n- If key already `SUCCEEDED`, worker short-circuits and reuses stored result.\n- First executor acquires/owns lock; success stores result in lock row.\n- Cooperative cancellation:\n- `pipeline_demo` executes step-wise and checks `cancel_requested` between steps.\n- On cancellation request during running, transition to `CANCELED` at next checkpoint.\n\n## Scheduler Behavior\n- Delayed jobs:\n- Poll DB for `state=QUEUED`, `enqueued=false`, `next_run_at<=now`, `cancel_requested=false`.\n- Enqueue to Redis stream and set `enqueued=true`, `last_stream_id`.\n- If crash after enqueue before DB update, duplicate enqueue is tolerated; worker CAS logic prevents duplicate execution.\n- Recurring jobs:\n- Load schedules from YAML/JSON on startup.\n- Use croniter in UTC to compute planned ticks.\n- For each planned tick, insert `schedule_ticks` unique key `(schedule_id, planned_time)`.\n- Only successful insert creates job and increments `scheduler_enqueues_total{schedule_id}`.\n- Restart-safe dedupe comes from `schedule_ticks` uniqueness.\n\n## Observability\n- Structured JSON logs for API/worker/scheduler/CLI.\n- Mandatory correlation fields emitted when available: `job_id`, `stream_id`, `consumer`, `attempt`, `state`.\n- Prometheus metrics exposed at `/metrics` with required metrics:\n- `jobs_enqueued_total{type,queue}`\n- `jobs_started_total{type}`\n- `jobs_finished_total{type,state}`\n- `job_duration_seconds{type}` histogram\n- `queue_depth{queue}` gauge\n- `worker_reclaims_total`\n- `scheduler_enqueues_total{schedule_id}`\n\n## Docker, Startup, and Developer Commands\n- `docker-compose.yml` services:\n- `redis` with persistence.\n- `api` on `:8000`.\n- `worker` (default queue `default`).\n- `scheduler` loading `config/schedules.yaml`.\n- Shared volume for SQLite DB path `/app/data/mini_celery.db`.\n- Commands:\n- `make up` -> `docker compose up --build -d`\n- `make down` -> `docker compose down -v`\n- `make lint` -> `ruff check . && ruff format --check . && mypy mini_celery`\n- `make test` -> compose-based test run with Redis + pytest.\n- `make loadtest` -> CLI loadtest against running API.\n- Fallback for no `make`: `python scripts/dev.py <up|down|lint|test|loadtest>`.\n\n## Automated Tests\n- Unit tests:\n- State transition rules including invalid transitions rejection.\n- Backoff/jitter bounds and monotonic growth under cap.\n- API idempotency behavior for non-terminal duplicates.\n- Worker idempotency short-circuit when prior success exists.\n- Integration tests:\n- At-least-once with crash: kill worker mid-job, second worker reclaims from PEL and completes.\n- DLQ after max attempts: retryable task exceeds attempts, ends in `DEAD`, entry appears in `jobs:dlq`.\n- Delayed execution: job with future `run_at` never runs early; runs after due time.\n- Scheduler restart dedupe: same `(schedule_id, planned_time)` enqueued once across restart.\n- `make test` exits non-zero on any failing unit/integration test.\n\n## README Content Plan\n- Architecture diagram and service responsibilities.\n- Reliability walkthrough for worker crash, Redis restart, scheduler restart.\n- Quickstart with exact commands.\n- API examples for each endpoint.\n- How to add a new task type (Pydantic payload + registry + tests).\n- Design tradeoffs section.\n- Assumptions section.\n\n## Assumptions and Defaults\n- UTC-only timestamps; RFC3339 accepted/emitted with timezone.\n- Manual retry resets `attempts` to `0`, clears `last_error`, preserves `id`, `type`, `payload`, `idempotency_key`.\n- Default queue name is `default`.\n- Default `max_attempts=3`.\n- Worker concurrency uses asyncio tasks, not multiprocessing.\n- Backoff defaults: `base=1s`, `multiplier=2.0`, `cap=30s`, `jitter_ratio=0.2`.\n- Visibility timeout default `30s`; reclaim interval default `5s`.\n- SQLite runs in WAL mode with busy timeout to reduce write contention.\n- Extra endpoint `GET /jobs/{job_id}/events` is added to support CLI tailing cleanly.\n- Because this environment lacks local `docker` and `make`, implementation will include script fallbacks while preserving required `make` targets and Docker-first workflow.\n", "type": "input_text"}], "role": "user", "type": "message"}, {"content": null, "encrypted_content": {"arguments": "{\"command\": \"@'\\n[build-system]\\nrequires = [\\\"hatchling>=1.24.0\\\"]\\nbuild-backend = \\\"hatchling.build\\\"\\n\\n[project]\\nname = \\\"mini-celery\\\"\\nversion = \\\"0.1.0\\\"\\ndescription = \\\"Local production-style job queue + scheduler using FastAPI, Redis Streams, and SQLite\\\"\\nreadme = \\\"README.md\\\"\\nrequires-python = \\\">=3.12\\\"\\nlicense = { text = \\\"MIT\\\" }\\nauthors = [{ name = \\\"mini-celery\\\" }]\\ndependencies = [\\n  \\\"fastapi>=0.115.0\\\",\\n  \\\"uvicorn[standard]>=0.31.0\\\",\\n  \\\"redis>=5.1.0\\\",\\n  \\\"SQLAlchemy>=2.0.36\\\",\\n  \\\"pydantic>=2.9.2\\\",\\n  \\\"pydantic-settings>=2.6.0\\\",\\n  \\\"httpx>=0.27.2\\\",\\n  \\\"prometheus-client>=0.21.0\\\",\\n  \\\"typer>=0.12.5\\\",\\n  \\\"croniter>=5.0.1\\\",\\n  \\\"PyYAML>=6.0.2\\\"\\n]\\n\\n[project.optional-dependencies]\\ndev = [\\n  \\\"pytest>=8.3.3\\\",\\n  \\\"pytest-asyncio>=0.24.0\\\",\\n  \\\"pytest-cov>=5.0.0\\\",\\n  \\\"ruff>=0.8.0\\\",\\n  \\\"mypy>=1.11.2\\\",\\n  \\\"types-PyYAML>=6.0.12.20240917\\\"\\n]\\n\\n[project.scripts]\\nmini-celery = \\\"mini_celery.cli:app\\\"\\n\\n[tool.pytest.ini_options]\\naddopts = \\\"-q\\\"\\nasyncio_mode = \\\"auto\\\"\\nmarkers = [\\n  \\\"integration: marks integration tests\\\"\\n]\\n\\n[tool.ruff]\\nline-length = 100\\ntarget-version = \\\"py312\\\"\\n\\n[tool.ruff.lint]\\nselect = [\\\"E\\\", \\\"F\\\", \\\"I\\\", \\\"UP\\\", \\\"B\\\", \\\"SIM\\\", \\\"RUF\\\"]\\n\\n[tool.ruff.format]\\nquote-style = \\\"double\\\"\\nindent-style = \\\"space\\\"\\n\\n[tool.mypy]\\npython_version = \\\"3.12\\\"\\nstrict = true\\nwarn_unused_ignores = true\\nwarn_redundant_casts = true\\nwarn_return_any = true\\ndisallow_any_generics = compose up --build -d\\n\\ndown:\\n\\tdocker compose down -v\\n\\nlint:\\n\\tpython -m ruff check REDACTED -m ruff format --check REDACTED -m mypy mini_celery\\n\\ntest:\\n\\tdocker compose -f docker-compose.test.yml up --build --abort-on-container-exit --exit-code-from test\\n\\tdocker compose -f docker-compose.test.yml down -v\\n\\nloadtest:\\n\\tpython -m mini_celery.cli loadtest --total 100 --concurrency 10 --task-type sleep --payload '{\\\"seconds\\\":0.05}'\\n'@ | Set-Content -Path Makefile\", \"workdir\": \"REDACTED\"}", "call_id": "REDACTED", "name": "shell_command", "type": "function_call"}, {"call_id": "REDACTED", "output": "Exit code: 0\nWall time: 0.2 seconds\nOutput:\n", "type": "function_call_output"}, {"arguments": "{\"command\": \"@'\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\n\\nimport argparse\\nimport subprocess\\nimport sys\\n\\n\\ndef run(cmd: list[str]) -> int:\\n    print(\\\"$\\\", \\\" \\\".join(cmd), flush=True)\\n    return subprocess.call(cmd)\\n\\n\\ndef main() -> int:\\n    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=\\\"Fallback task runner when make is unavailable\\\")\\n    parser.add_argument(\\\"command\\\", choices=[\\\"up\\\", \\\"down\\\", \\\"lint\\\", \\\"test\\\", \\\"loadtest\\\"])\\n    args = parser.parse_args()\\n\\n    if args.command == \\\"up\\\":\\n        return run([\\\"docker\\\", \\\"compose\\\", \\\"up\\\", \\\"--build\\\", \\\"-d\\\"])\\n    if args.command == \\\"down\\\":\\n        return run([\\\"docker\\\", \\\"compose\\\", \\\"down\\\", \\\"-v\\\"])\\n    if args.command == \\\"lint\\\":\\n        steps = [\\n            [sys.executable, \\\"-m\\\", \\\"ruff\\\", \\\"check\\\", \\\".\\\"],\\n            [sys.executable, \\\"-m\\\", \\\"ruff\\\", \\\"format\\\", \\\"--check\\\", \\\".\\\"],\\n            [sys.executable, \\\"-m\\\", \\\"mypy\\\", \\\"mini_celery\\\"],\\n        ]\\n        for step in steps:\\n            code = run(step)\\n            if code:\\n                return code\\n        return 0\\n    if args.command == \\\"test\\\":\\n        code = run(\\n            [\\n                \\\"docker\\\",\\n                \\\"compose\\\",\\n                \\\"-f\\\",\\n                \\\"docker-compose.test.yml\\\",\\n                \\\"up\\\",\\n                \\\"--build\\\",\\n                \\\"--abort-on-container-exit\\\",\\n                \\\"--exit-code-from\\\",\\n                \\\"test\\\",\\n            ]\\n        )\\n        down_code = run([\\\"docker\\\", \\\"compose\\\", \\\"-f\\\", \\\"docker-compose.test.yml\\\", \\\"down\\\", \\\"-v\\\"])\\n        return code or down_code\\n    if args.command == \\\"loadtest\\\":\\n        return run(\\n            [\\n                sys.executable,\\n                \\\"-m\\\",\\n                \\\"mini_celery.cli\\\",\\n                \\\"loadtest\\\",\\n                \\\"--total\\\",\\n                \\\"100\\\",\\n                \\\"--concurrency\\\",\\n                \\\"10\\\",\\n                \\\"--task-type\\\",\\n                \\\"sleep\\\",\\n                \\\"--payload\\\",\\n                '{\\\"seconds\\\":0.05}',\\n            ]\\n        )\\n\\n    return 1\\n\\n\\nif __name__ == \\\"__main__\\\":\\n    raise SystemExit(main())\\n'@ | Set-Content -Path scripts\\\\dev.py\", \"workdir\": \"REDACTED\"}", "call_id": "REDACTED", "name": "shell_command", "type": "function_call"}, {"call_id": "REDACTED", "output": "Exit code: 0\nWall time: 0.2 seconds\nOutput:\n", "type": "summary": [{"text": "**Creating configuration with pydantic-settings**", "type": "summary_text"}], "type": "reasoning"}, {"arguments": "{\"command\": \"@'\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\n\\nfrom functools import lru_cache\\n\\nfrom pydantic import Field\\nfrom pydantic_settings import BaseSettings, SettingsConfigDict\\n\\n\\nclass Settings(BaseSettings):\\n    model_config = SettingsConfigDict(env_file=\\\".env\\\", extra=\\\"ignore\\\")\\n\\n    app_name: str = \\\"mini-celery\\\"\\n    log_level: str = \\\"INFO\\\"\\n\\n    database_url: str = \\\"sqlite:///REDACTED\\\"\\n    redis_url: str = \\\"redis://localhost:6379/0\\\"\\n\\n    stream_prefix: str = \\\"jobs\\\"\\n    consumer_group: str = \\\"workers\\\"\\n    default_queue: str = \\\"default\\\"\\n    worker_queues: str = \\\"default\\\"\\n\\n    worker_concurrency: int = 4\\n    visibility_timeout_sec: int = 30\\n    reclaim_interval_sec: int = 5\\n\\n    scheduler_poll_interval_sec: float = 1.0\\n    scheduler_config_path: str = \\\"config/schedules.yaml\\\"\\n\\n    retry_base_delay_sec: float = 1.0\\n    retry_multiplier: float = 2.0\\n    retry_cap_delay_sec: float = 30.0\\n    retry_jitter_ratio: float = 0.2\\n\\n    api_url: str = Field(default=\\\"http://localhost:8000\\\", alias=\\\"API_URL\\\")\\n\\n    def stream_name(self, queue: str) -> str:\\n        return f\\\"{self.stream_prefix}:{queue}\\\"\\n\\n    @property\\n    def dlq_stream_name(self) -> str:\\n        return f\\\"{self.stream_prefix}:dlq\\\"\\n\\n    @property\\n    def queues(self) -> list[str]:\\n        return [q.strip() for q in self.worker_queues.split(\\\",\\\") if q.strip()]\\n\\n\\n@lru_cache(maxsize=1)\\ndef get_settings() -> Settings:\\n    return Settings()\\n\\n\\ndef reset_settings_cache() -> None:\\n    get_settings.cache_clear()\\n'@ | Set-Content -Path mini_celery\\\\config.py\", \"workdir\": \"REDACTED\"}", "call_id": "summary": [{"text": "**Creating structured JSON logger**", "type": "summary_text"}], "type": "reasoning"}, {"arguments": "{\"command\": \"@'\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\n\\nimport json\\nimport logging\\nfrom datetime import UTC, datetime\\nfrom typing import Any\\n\\n\\nclass JsonLogFormatter(logging.Formatter):\\n    def format(self, record: logging.LogRecord) -> str:\\n        base: dict[str, Any] = {\\n            \\\"ts\\\": datetime.now(UTC).isoformat(),\\n            \\\"level\\\": record.levelname,\\n            \\\"logger\\\": record.name,\\n            \\\"message\\\": record.getMessage(),\\n        }\\n\\n        if record.exc_info:\\n            base[\\\"exc_info\\\"] = self.formatException(record.exc_info)\\n\\n        standard = {\\n            \\\"name\\\",\\n            \\\"msg\\\",\\n            \\\"args\\\",\\n            \\\"levelname\\\",\\n            \\\"levelno\\\",\\n            \\\"pathname\\\",\\n            \\\"filename\\\",\\n            \\\"module\\\",\\n            \\\"exc_info\\\",\\n            \\\"exc_text\\\",\\n            \\\"stack_info\\\",\\n            \\\"lineno\\\",\\n            \\\"funcName\\\",\\n            \\\"created\\\",\\n            \\\"msecs\\\",\\n            \\\"relativeCreated\\\",\\n            \\\"thread\\\",\\n            \\\"threadName\\\",\\n            \\\"processName\\\",\\n            \\\"process\\\",\\n            \\\"taskName\\\",\\n        }\\n        for key, value in record.__dict__.items():\\n            if key not in standard and not key.startswith(\\\"_\\\"):\\n                base[key] = value\\n\\n        return json.dumps(base, default=str)\\n\\n\\ndef setup_logging(level: str = \\\"INFO\\\") -> None:\\n    root = logging.getLogger()\\n    root.setLevel(level.upper())\\n    handler = logging.StreamHandler()\\n    handler.setFormatter(JsonLogFormatter())\\n    root.handlers.clear()\\n    root.addHandler(handler)\\n'@ | Set-Content -Path mini_celery\\\\logging.py\", \"workdir\": \"REDACTED\"}", "call_id": "REDACTED", "name": "shell_command", "type": "function_call"}, {"call_id": "REDACTED", "output": "Exit code: 0\nWall time: 0.2 \\\"queue\\\"])\\njobs_started_total = Counter(\\\"jobs_started_total\\\", \\\"Jobs started\\\", [\\\"type\\\"])\\njobs_finished_total = Counter(\\\"jobs_finished_total\\\", \\\"Jobs finished\\\", [\\\"type\\\", \\\"state\\\"])\\njob_duration_seconds = Histogram(\\\"job_duration_seconds\\\", \\\"Job execution duration\\\", [\\\"type\\\"])\\nqueue_depth = Gauge(\\\"queue_depth\\\", \\\"Queue depth\\\", [\\\"queue\\\"])\\nworker_reclaims_total = Counter(\\\"worker_reclaims_total\\\", \\\"Reclaimed pending messages\\\")\\nscheduler_enqueues_total = Counter(\\n "summary": [{"text": "**Creating typed SQLAlchemy models**", "type": "summary_text"}], "type": "reasoning"}, {"arguments": "{\"command\": \"@'\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\n\\nfrom datetime import UTC, datetime\\nfrom enum import Enum\\nfrom typing import Any\\nfrom uuid import uuid4\\n\\nfrom sqlalchemy import Boolean, DateTime, Integer, JSON, String, Text, UniqueConstraint\\nfrom sqlalchemy.orm import DeclarativeBase, Mapped, mapped_column\\n\\n\\nclass Base(DeclarativeBase):\\n    pass\\n\\n\\nclass JobState(str, Enum):\\n    QUEUED = \\\"QUEUED\\\"\\n    RUNNING = \\\"RUNNING\\\"\\n    SUCCEEDED = \\\"SUCCEEDED\\\"\\n    FAILED = \\\"FAILED\\\"\\n    CANCELED = \\\"CANCELED\\\"\\n    DEAD = \\\"DEAD\\\"\\n\\n\\nTERMINAL_STATES: set[JobState] = {\\n    JobState.SUCCEEDED,\\n    JobState.FAILED,\\n    JobState.CANCELED,\\n    JobState.DEAD,\\n}\\n\\n\\nclass IdempotencyStatus(str, Enum):\\n    IN_PROGRESS = \\\"IN_PROGRESS\\\"\\n    SUCCEEDED = \\\"SUCCEEDED\\\"\\n\\n\\ndef utc_now() -> datetime:\\n    return datetime.now(UTC)\\n\\n\\nclass Job(Base):\\n    __tablename__ = \\\"jobs\\\"\\n\\n    id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(36), primary_key=True, default=lambda: str(uuid4()))\\n    type: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(100), index=True)\\n    payload: Mapped[dict[str, Any]] = mapped_column(JSON)\\n    state: Mapped[JobState] = mapped_column(String(20), index=True)\\n    created_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(DateTime(timezone=True), default=utc_now, index=True)\\n    updated_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(DateTime(timezone=True), default=utc_now, onupdate=utc_now)\\n\\n    idempotency_key: Mapped[str | None] = mapped_column(String(200), nullable=True, index=True)\\n    attempts: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(Integer, default=0)\\n    max_attempts: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(Integer, default=3)\\n\\n    next_run_at: Mapped[datetime | None] = mapped_column(DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=True, index=True)\\n    schedule: Mapped[str | None] = mapped_column(String(200), nullable=True)\\n\\n    last_error: Mapped[str | None] = mapped_column(Text, nullable=True)\\n    result: Mapped[dict[str, Any] | None] = mapped_column(JSON, nullable=True)\\n\\n    queue: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(100), default=\\\"default\\\", index=True)\\n    cancel_requested: Mapped[bool] = mapped_column(Boolean, default=False)\\n    lease_expires_at: Mapped[datetime | None] = mapped_column(DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=True)\\n    last_stream_id: Mapped[str | None] = mapped_column(String(64), nullable=True)\\n    enqueued: Mapped[bool] = mapped_column(Boolean, default=False, index=True)\\n    version: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(Integer, default=0)\\n\\n\\nclass JobEvent(Base):\\n    __tablename__ = \\\"job_events\\\"\\n\\n    id: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)\\n    job_id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(36), index=True)\\n    ts: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(DateTime(timezone=True), default=utc_now, index=True)\\n    old_state: Mapped[str | None] = "summary": [{"text": "**Creating database module**", "type": "summary_text"}], "type": "reasoning"}, {"arguments": "{\"command\": \"@'\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\n\\nfrom contextlib import contextmanager\\nfrom typing import Iterator\\n\\nfrom sqlalchemy import create_engine, event, text\\nfrom sqlalchemy.engine import Engine\\nfrom sqlalchemy.orm import Session, sessionmaker\\n\\nfrom mini_celery.config import Settings, get_settings\\nfrom mini_celery.models import Base\\n\\n\\nclass Database:\\n    def __init__(self, database_url: str):\\n        connect_args: dict[str, object] = {}\\n        if database_url.startswith(\\\"sqlite\\\"):\\n            connect_args = {\\\"check_same_thread\\\": False}\\n\\n        self.engine = create_engine(database_url, connect_args=connect_args, future=True)\\n        self.SessionLocal = sessionmaker(\\n            bind=self.engine,\\n            class_=Session,\\n            autocommit=False,\\n            autoflush=False,\\n            expire_on_commit=False,\\n        )\\n\\n        if database_url.startswith(\\\"sqlite\\\"):\\n\\n            @event.listens_for(self.engine, \\\"connect\\\")\\n            def _set_sqlite_pragma(dbapi_connection: object, _: object) -> None:\\n                cursor = dbapi_connection.cursor()\\n                cursor.execute(\\\"PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL;\\\")\\n                cursor.execute(\\\"PRAGMA busy_timeout=5000;\\\")\\n                cursor.execute(\\\"PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON;\\\")\\n                cursor.close()\\n\\n    def init(self) -> None:\\n        Base.metadata.create_all(bind=self.engine)\\n\\n    @contextmanager\\n    def session(self) -> Iterator[Session]:\\n        session = self.SessionLocal()\\n        try:\\n            yield session\\n            session.commit()\\n        except Exception:\\n            session.rollback()\\n            raise\\n        finally:\\n            session.close()\\n\\n    def check(self) -> bool:\\n        with self.session() as session:\\n            session.execute(text(\\\"SELECT 1\\\"))\\n        return True\\n\\n\\n_default_settings: Settings = get_settings()\\ndefault_db = Database(_default_settings.database_url)\\n\\n\\ndef get_db() -> Database:\\n    return default_db\\n\\n\\ndef get_engine() -> Engine:\\n    return default_db.engine\\n'@ | Set-Content -Path "summary": [{"text": "**Creating state machine foundation**", "type": "summary_text"}], "type": "reasoning"}, {"arguments": "{\"command\": \"@'\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\n\\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\\n\\nfrom mini_celery.models import JobState\\n\\n\\nclass InvalidTransition(ValueError):\\n    pass\\n\\n\\nALLOWED_TRANSITIONS: dict[JobState, set[JobState]] = {\\n    JobState.QUEUED: {JobState.RUNNING, JobState.CANCELED},\\n    JobState.RUNNING: {\\n        JobState.SUCCEEDED,\\n        JobState.FAILED,\\n        JobState.CANCELED,\\n        JobState.DEAD,\\n        JobState.QUEUED,\\n    },\\n    JobState.FAILED: {JobState.QUEUED},\\n    JobState.DEAD: {JobState.QUEUED},\\n    JobState.SUCCEEDED: set(),\\n    JobState.CANCELED: set(),\\n}\\n\\n\\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\\nclass Transition:\\n    old: JobState\\n    new: JobState\\n\\n\\ndef can_transition(old: JobState, new: JobState) -> bool:\\n    return new in ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS.get(old, set())\\n\\n\\ndef assert_transition(old: JobState, new: JobState) -> None:\\n    if not can_transition(old, new):\\n        raise InvalidTransition(f\\\"Invalid transition: {old} -> {new}\\\")\\n'@ | Set-Content -Path mini_celery\\\\state_machine.py\", \"workdir\": \"REDACTED\"}", "call_id": "REDACTED", "name": "shell_command", "type": "function_call"}, "summary": [{"text": "**Planning backoff module**", "type": "summary_text"}], "type": "reasoning"}, {"arguments": "{\"command\": \"@'\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\n\\nimport random\\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\\n\\n\\n@dataclass(slots=True)\\nclass BackoffPolicy:\\n    base_delay_sec: float = 1.0\\n    multiplier: float = 2.0\\n    cap_delay_sec: float = 30.0\\n    jitter_ratio: float = 0.2\\n\\n    def compute_delay(self, attempt: int, rng: random.Random | None = None) -> float:\\n        if attempt < 1:\\n            raise ValueError(\\\"attempt must be >= 1\\\")\\n        effective_rng = rng or random.Random()\\n\\n        base = min(self.cap_delay_sec, self.base_delay_sec * (self.multiplier ** (attempt - 1)))\\n        jitter_span = base * self.jitter_ratio\\n        jitter = (effective_rng.random() * 2.0 - 1.0) * jitter_span\\n        delay = max(0.0, base + jitter)\\n        return delay\\n'@ | Set-Content -Path mini_celery\\\\backoff.py\", \"workdir\": annotations\\n\\nfrom datetime import datetime\\nfrom typing import Any\\nfrom uuid import UUID\\n\\nfrom pydantic import BaseModel, ConfigDict, Field\\n\\nfrom mini_celery.models import Job, JobEvent, JobState\\n\\n\\nclass CreateJobRequest(BaseModel):\\n    type: str\\n    payload: dict[str, Any]\\n    queue: str = \\\"default\\\"\\n    max_attempts: int = Field(default=3, ge=1, le=100)\\n    run_at: datetime | None = None\\n    idempotency_key: str | None = Field(default=None, max_length=200)\\n\\n\\nclass CreateJobResponse(BaseModel):\\n    job_id: UUID\\n\\n\\nclass JobResponse(BaseModel):\\n    model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)\\n\\n    id: UUID\\n    type: str\\n    payload: dict[str, Any]\\n    state: JobState\\n    created_at: datetime\\n    updated_at: datetime\\n    idempotency_key: str | None = None\\n    attempts: int\\n    max_attempts: int\\n    next_run_at: datetime | None = None\\n    schedule: str | None = None\\n    last_error: str | None = None\\n    result: dict[str, Any] | None = None\\n    queue: str\\n    cancel_requested: bool\\n    lease_expires_at: datetime | None = None\\n    last_stream_id: str | None = None\\n    enqueued: bool\\n    version: int\\n\\n\\nclass JobEventResponse(BaseModel):\\n    model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)\\n\\n    id: int\\n    job_id: UUID\\n    ts: datetime\\n    old_state: str | None\\n    new_state: str\\n    message: str\\n    metadata: dict[str, Any] | None = None\\n\\n\\nclass JobsListResponse(BaseModel):\\n    items: list[JobResponse]\\n    next_cursor: str | None = None\\n\\n\\nclass JobEventsListResponse(BaseModel):\\n    items: list[JobEventResponse]\\n    next_cursor_ts: datetime | None = None\\n\\n\\nclass ActionJobResponse(BaseModel):\\n    job: JobResponse\\n\\n\\ndef job_to_response(job: Job) -> JobResponse:\\n    return JobResponse.model_validate(job)\\n\\n\\ndef jobs_to_response(jobs: list[Job]) -> list[JobResponse]:\\n    return [job_to_response(job) for job in jobs]\\n\\n\\ndef events_to_response(events: list[JobEvent]) -> list[JobEventResponse]:\\n    return [JobEventResponse.model_validate(event) for event in events]\\n'@ "summary": [{"text": "**Designing robust Redis stream parsing**", "type": "summary_text"}], "type": "reasoning"}, {"arguments": "{\"command\": \"@'\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\n\\nfrom collections.abc import Mapping\\n\\nfrom redis.asyncio import Redis\\nfrom redis.exceptions import ResponseError\\n\\nfrom mini_celery.broker.base import Broker\\nfrom mini_celery.config import Settings, get_settings\\n\\n\\nclass RedisStreamsBroker(Broker):\\n    def __init__(self, settings: Settings | None = None, redis_client: Redis | None = None):\\n        self.settings = settings or get_settings()\\n        self.redis: Redis = redis_client or Redis.from_url(\\n            self.settings.redis_url,\\n            decode_responses=True,\\n        )\\n\\n    def _stream(self, queue: str) -> str:\\n        return self.settings.stream_name(queue)\\n\\n    async def ensure_consumer_group(self, queue: str) -> None:\\n        stream = self._stream(queue)\\n        try:\\n            await self.redis.xgroup_create(\\n                name=stream,\\n                groupname=self.settings.consumer_group,\\n                id=\\\"0\\\",\\n                mkstream=True,\\n            )\\n        except ResponseError as exc:\\n            if \\\"BUSYGROUP\\\" not in str(exc):\\n                raise\\n\\n    async def enqueue(self, queue: str, fields: Mapping[str, str]) -> str:\\n        await self.ensure_consumer_group(queue)\\n        stream = self._stream(queue)\\n        return await self.redis.xadd(stream, fields)  # type: ignore[return-value]\\n\\n    async def read_group(\\n        self,\\n        queue: str,\\n        consumer: str,\\n        count: int,\\n        block_ms: int,\\n    ) -> list[tuple[str, dict[str, str]]]:\\n        await self.ensure_consumer_group(queue)\\n        stream = self._stream(queue)\\n        response = await self.redis.xreadgroup(\\n            groupname=self.settings.consumer_group,\\n            consumername=consumer,\\n            streams={stream: \\\">\\\"},\\n            count=count,\\n            block=block_ms,\\n        )\\n        results: list[tuple[str, dict[str, str]]] = []\\n        for _, entries in response:\\n            for stream_id, fields in entries:\\n                normalized = {str(k): str(v) for k, v in fields.items()}\\n                results.append((stream_id, normalized))\\n        return results\\n\\n    async def ack(self, queue: str, stream_id: str) -> int:\\n        stream = self._stream(queue)\\n        return await self.redis.xack(stream, self.settings.consumer_group, stream_id)\\n\\n    async def claim_stale(\\n        self,\\n        queue: str,\\n        consumer: str,\\n        min_idle_ms: int,\\n        count: int,\\n    ) -> list[tuple[str, dict[str, str]]]:\\n        stream = self._stream(queue)\\n        await self.ensure_consumer_group(queue)\\n\\n        pending = await self.redis.xpending_range(\\n            name=stream,\\n            groupname=self.settings.consumer_group,\\n            min=\\\"-\\\",\\n            max=\\\"+\\\",\\n            count=count,\\n            idle=min_idle_ms,\\n        )\\n        if not pending:\\n            return []\\n\\n        message_ids = [item[\\\"message_id\\\"] for item in pending]\\n        claimed = await self.redis.xclaim(\\n            name=stream,\\n            groupname=self.settings.consumer_group,\\n            consumername=consumer,\\n            min_idle_time=min_idle_ms,\\n            message_ids=message_ids,\\n        )\\n\\n        results: list[tuple[str, dict[str, str]]] = []\\n        for stream_id, fields in claimed:\\n            normalized = {str(k): str(v) for k, v in fields.items()}\\n            results.append((stream_id, normalized))\\n        return results\\n\\n    async def queue_depth(self, queue: str) -> int:\\n        stream = self._stream(queue)\\n        return await self.redis.xlen(stream)\\n\\n    async def publish_dlq(self, fields: Mapping[str, str]) -> str:\\n        return await self.redis.xadd(self.settings.dlq_stream_name, fields)  # type: ignore[return-value]\\n\\n    async def ping(self) -> bool:\\n        response = await self.redis.ping()\\n        return bool(response)\\n\\n    async def close(self) -> None:\\n        await self.redis.aclose()\\n'@ | Set-Content -Path 0\nWall time: 0.2 seconds\nOutput:\n", "type": "function_call_output"}], "instructions": "You are Codex, a coding agent based on GPT-5. You and the user share the same workspace and collaborate to achieve the user's goals.\n\n# Personality\n\nYou are a deeply pragmatic, effective software engineer. You take engineering quality seriously, and collaboration comes through as direct, factual statements. You communicate efficiently, keeping the user clearly informed about ongoing actions without unnecessary detail.\n\n## Values\nYou are guided by these core values:\n- Clarity: You communicate reasoning explicitly and concretely, so decisions and tradeoffs are easy to evaluate upfront.\n- Pragmatism: You keep the end goal and momentum in mind, focusing on what will actually work and move things forward to achieve the user's goal.\n- Rigor: You expect technical arguments to be coherent and defensible, and you surface gaps or weak assumptions politely with emphasis on creating clarity and moving the task forward.\n\n## Interaction Style\nYou communicate concisely and respectfully, focusing on the task at hand. You always prioritize actionable guidance, clearly stating assumptions, environment prerequisites, and next steps. Unless explicitly asked, you avoid excessively verbose explanations about your work.\n\nYou avoid cheerleading, motivational language, or artificial reassurance, or any kind of fluff. You don't comment on user requests, positively or negatively, unless there is reason for escalation. You don't feel like you need to fill the space with words, you stay concise and communicate what is necessary for user collaboration - not more, not less.\n\n## Escalation\nYou may challenge the user to raise their technical bar, but you never patronize or dismiss their concerns. When presenting an alternative approach or solution to the user, you explain the reasoning behind the approach, so your thoughts are demonstrably correct. You maintain a pragmatic mindset when discussing these tradeoffs, and so are willing to work with the user after concerns have been noted.\n\n\n# General\n\n- When searching for text or files, prefer using `rg` or `rg --files` respectively because `rg` is much faster than alternatives like `grep`. (If the `rg` command is not found, then use alternatives.)\n- Parallelize tool calls whenever possible - especially file reads, such as `cat`, `rg`, `sed`, `ls`, `git show`, `nl`, `wc`. Use `multi_tool_use.parallel` to parallelize tool calls and only this.\n\n## Editing constraints\n\n- Default to ASCII when editing or creating files. Only introduce non-ASCII or other Unicode characters when there is a clear justification and the file already uses them.\n- Add succinct code comments that explain what is going on if code is not self-explanatory. You should not add comments like \"Assigns the value to the variable\", but a brief comment might be useful ahead of a complex code block that the user would otherwise have to spend time parsing out. Usage of these comments should be rare.\n- Try to use apply_patch for single file edits, but it is fine to explore other options to make the edit if it does not work well. Do not use apply_patch for changes that are auto-generated (i.e. generating package.json or running a lint or format command like gofmt) or when scripting is more efficient (such as search and replacing a string across a codebase).\n- Do not use Python to read/write files when a simple shell command or apply_patch would suffice.\n- You may be in a dirty git worktree.\n    * NEVER revert existing changes you did not make unless explicitly requested, since these changes were made by the user.\n    * If asked to make a commit or code edits and there are unrelated changes to your work or changes that you didn't make in those files, don't revert those changes.\n    * If the changes are in files you've touched recently, you should read carefully and understand how you can work with the changes rather than reverting them.\n    * If the changes are in unrelated files, just ignore them and don't revert them.\n- Do not amend a commit unless explicitly requested to do so.\n- While you are working, you might notice unexpected changes that you didn't make. If this happens, STOP IMMEDIATELY and ask the user how they would like to proceed.\n- **NEVER** use destructive commands like `git reset --hard` or `git checkout --` unless specifically requested or approved by the user.\n- You struggle using the git interactive console. **ALWAYS** prefer using non-interactive git commands.\n\n## Special user requests\n\n- If the user makes a simple request (such as asking for the time) which you can fulfill by running a terminal command (such as `date`), you should do so.\n- If the user asks for a \"review\", default to a code review mindset: prioritise identifying bugs, risks, behavioural regressions, and missing tests. Findings must be the primary focus of the response - keep summaries or overviews brief and only after enumerating the issues. Present findings first (ordered by severity with file/line references), follow with open questions or assumptions, and offer a change-summary only as a secondary detail. If no findings are discovered, state that explicitly and mention any residual risks or testing gaps.\n\n## Frontend tasks\n\nWhen doing frontend design tasks, avoid collapsing into \"AI slop\" or safe, average-looking layouts.\nAim for interfaces that feel intentional, bold, and a bit surprising.\n- Typography: Use expressive, purposeful fonts and avoid default stacks (Inter, Roboto, Arial, system).\n- Color & Look: Choose a clear visual direction; define CSS variables; avoid purple-on-white defaults. No purple bias or dark mode bias.\n- Motion: Use a few meaningful animations (page-load, staggered reveals) instead of generic micro-motions.\n- Background: Don't rely on flat, single-color backgrounds; use gradients, shapes, or subtle patterns to build atmosphere.\n- Overall: Avoid boilerplate layouts and interchangeable UI patterns. Vary themes, type families, and visual languages across outputs.\n- Ensure the page loads properly on both desktop and mobile\n\nException: If working within an existing website or design system, preserve the established patterns, structure, and visual language.\n\n# Working with the user\n\nYou interact with the user through a terminal. You have 2 ways of communicating with the users:\n- Share intermediary updates in `commentary` channel. \n- After you have completed all your work, send a message to the `final` channel.\nYou are producing plain text that will later be styled by the program you run in. Formatting should make results easy to scan, but not feel mechanical. Use judgment to decide how much structure adds value. Follow the formatting rules exactly.\n\n## Autonomy and persistence\nPersist until the task is fully handled end-to-end within the current turn whenever feasible: do not stop at analysis or partial fixes; carry changes through implementation, verification, and a clear explanation of outcomes unless the user explicitly pauses or redirects you.\n\nUnless the user explicitly asks for a plan, asks a question about the code, is brainstorming potential solutions, or some other intent that makes it clear that code should not be written, assume the user wants you to make code changes or run tools to solve the user's problem. In these cases, it's bad to output your proposed solution in a message, you should go ahead and actually implement the change. If you encounter challenges or blockers, you should attempt to resolve them yourself.\n\n## Formatting rules\n\n- You may format with GitHub-flavored Markdown.\n- Structure your answer if necessary, the complexity of the answer should match the task. If the task is simple, your answer should be a one-liner. Order sections from general to specific to supporting.\n- Never use nested bullets. Keep lists flat (single level). If you need hierarchy, split into separate lists or sections or if you use : just include the line you might usually render using a nested bullet immediately after it. For numbered lists, only use the `1. 2. 3.` style markers (with a period), never `1)`.\n- Headers are optional, only use them when you think they are necessary. If you do use them, use short Title Case (1-3 words) wrapped in **…**. Don't add a blank line.\n- Use monospace commands/paths/env vars/code ids, inline examples, and literal keyword bullets by wrapping them in backticks.\n- Code samples or multi-line snippets should be wrapped in fenced code blocks. Include an info string as often as possible.\n- File References: When referencing files in your response follow the below rules:\n  * Use markdown links (not inline code) for clickable files.\n  * Each file reference should have a stand-alone path; use inline code for non-clickable paths (for example, directories).\n  * For clickable/openable file references, the path target must be an absolute filesystem path. Labels may be short (for example, `[app.ts](/abs/path/app.ts)`).\n  * Optionally include line/column (1‑based): :line[:column] or #Lline[Ccolumn] (column defaults to 1).\n  * Do not use URIs like file://, vscode://, or https://.\n  * Do not provide range of lines\n  * Examples: src/app.ts, src/app.ts:42, b/server/index.js#L10, REDACTED\n- Don’t use emojis or em dashes unless explicitly instructed.\n\n## Final answer instructions\n\n- Balance conciseness to not overwhelm the user with appropriate detail for the request. Do not narrate abstractly; explain what you are doing and why.\n- Do not begin responses with conversational interjections or meta commentary. Avoid openers such as acknowledgements (“Done —”, “Got it”, “Great question, ”) or framing phrases.\n- The user does not see command execution outputs. When asked to show the output of a command (e.g. `git show`), relay the important details in your answer or summarize the key lines so the user understands the result.\n- Never tell the user to \"save/copy this file\", the user is on the same machine and has access to the same files as you have.\n- If the user asks for a code explanation, structure your answer with code references.\n- When given a simple task, just provide the outcome in a short answer without strong formatting.\n- When you make big or complex changes, state the solution first, then walk the user through what you did and why.\n- For casual chit-chat, just chat.\n- If you weren't able to do something, for example run tests, tell the user.\n- If there are natural next steps the user may want to take, suggest them at the end of your response. Do not make suggestions if there are no natural next steps. When suggesting multiple options, use numeric lists for the suggestions so the user can quickly respond with a single number.\n\n## Intermediary updates \n\n- Intermediary updates go to the `commentary` channel.\n- User updates are short updates while you are working, they are NOT final answers.\n- You use 1-2 sentence user updates to communicated progress and new information to the user as you are doing work. \n- Do not begin responses with conversational interjections or meta commentary. Avoid openers such as acknowledgements (“Done —”, “Got it”, “Great question, ”) or framing phrases.\n- You provide user updates frequently, every 20s.\n- Before exploring or doing substantial work, you start with a user update acknowledging the request and explaining your first step. You should include your understanding of the user request and explain what you will do. Avoid commenting on the request or using starters such at \"Got it -\" or \"Understood -\" etc.\n- When exploring, e.g. searching, reading files you provide user updates as you go, every 20s, explaining what context you are gathering and what you've learned. Vary your sentence structure when providing these updates to avoid sounding repetitive - in particular, don't start each sentence the same way.\n- After you have sufficient context, and the work is substantial you provide a longer plan (this is the only user update that may be longer than 2 sentences and can contain formatting).\n- Before performing file edits of any kind, you provide updates explaining what edits you are making.\n- As you are thinking, you very frequently provide updates even if not taking any actions, informing the user of your progress. You interrupt your thinking and send multiple updates in a row if thinking for more than 100 words.\n- Tone of your updates MUST match your personality.\n", "model": "gpt-5.3-codex", "parallel_tool_calls": true, "prompt_cache_key": "019cbc1f-e440-72b2-a9a8-c0508695c2d9", "reasoning": {"effort": "xhigh", "summary": "auto"}, "store": false, "stream": true, "text": {"verbosity": "low"}, "tool_choice": "auto", "tools": [{"description": "Runs a Powershell command (Windows) and returns its output.\n\nExamples of valid command strings:\n\n- ls -a (show hidden): \"Get-ChildItem -Force\"\n- recursive find by name: \"Get-ChildItem -Recurse -Filter *.py\"\n- recursive grep: \"Get-ChildItem -Path REDACTED'TODO' -CaseSensitive\"\n- ps aux | grep python: \"Get-Process | Where-Object { $_.ProcessName -like '*python*' }\"\n- setting an env var: \"$env:FOO='bar'; echo $env:FOO\"\n- running an inline Python script: \"@'\\\\nprint('Hello, world!')\\\\n'@ | python -", "name": "shell_command", "parameters": {"additionalProperties": false, "properties": {"command": {"description": "The shell script to execute in the user's default shell", "type": "string"}, "justification": {"description": "Only set if sandbox_permissions is \\\"require_escalated\\\".\n                    Request approval from the user to run this command outside the sandbox.\n                    Phrased as a simple question that summarizes the purpose of the\n                    command as it relates to the task at hand - e.g. 'Do you want to\n                    fetch and pull the latest version of this git branch?'", "type": "string"}, "login": {"description": "Whether to run the shell with login shell semantics.
